content:
# What are Legacy voices? – ElevenLabs

Legacy voices are former Default voices that do not meet the current quality standard of our Default voice lineup. They will be deprecated on **28 February 2026** and removed from all products after that date.

While we understand this change may be disruptive, we are replacing Legacy voices with new, higher quality Default voices that will remain available in perpetuity.

If you use the API, your existing Legacy voice IDs will automatically route to their replacement Default voice IDs.

While we have replaced Legacy voices with similar voices, the new options may differ slightly in tone and style. We encourage you to explore additional options using these tools:

*   [**Voice Design**](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product-guides/voices/voice-design)**:** describe the voice you want to create using our voice design prompt.
    
*   [**Voice remixing**](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/capabilities/voice-remixing)**:** edit an existing voice’s style and tone using our best practices guide.
    
*   [**Instant Voice Clone**](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product-guides/voices/voice-cloning/instant-voice-cloning)**:** clone a voice using as little as 10 seconds of audio.
    
*   [**Professional Voice Clone**](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product-guides/voices/voice-cloning/professional-voice-cloning)**:** create a high fidelity clone of your voice with as little as 10 minutes of audio.
    
*   [**Voice Library**](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product-guides/voices/voice-library)**:** choose from a collection of Professional Voice Clones shared by the ElevenLabs community. You can also search the library using an audio or video clip to find similar voices.
    

Between now and 28 February 2026, Legacy voices will remain available to users who have previously used them. These users will continue to see and use their Legacy voices until the deprecation date. A reminder email will be sent one month before removal.

Legacy voices will not appear in voice selection menus by default. If you’ve used them before, you can locate them by:

*   Checking the **Recently Used** section.
    
*   Searching by name and enabling “Show legacy voices” in **Voices > Default Voices**.
    

To find the Voice ID for a Legacy voice:

*   **On the website:** open **Voices > Default Voices**, find your voice, then select **More actions** (three dots) **> Copy voice ID**.
    
*   **Via API:** send a GET request to the List Voices endpoint with the `show_legacy` flag enabled. See the [API Reference](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/voices/search) for full details.
    

These Legacy voices will be deprecated on **28 February 2026**:

content:
# What are Default voices? – ElevenLabs

Our Default voices are a curated set of voices optimized for our core use cases and made available to all ElevenLabs users by default.

All our Default voices are trained on Flash v2 and v2.5, Turbo v2 and v2.5, and Multilingual v2 models.  

If you are using the API, you can find the voice ID for Default voices in [My Voices](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-lab). First find the voice in your list of voices, click the **More actions** (three dots) button, then **Copy voice ID**. The voice ID will be automatically copied to your clipboard.

You can also get the voice ID for Default voices by sending a GET request to the **List voices** endpoint.  Please see the [API Reference](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/voices/search) for full details.

Default voices can't be deleted, but they don't take up your custom voice slots.

content:
# How do I delete voices from My Voices? – ElevenLabs

To delete a voice from [My Voices](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-lab), first find it in your list of voices, click the **More actions** (three dots) button, then select **Delete voice.**

![Screenshot 2025-06-06 103426.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/35961018174609)

Deleting a voice cannot be undone, and you will be asked to confirm the deletion.

Default and Legacy voices cannot be deleted, but these voices do not take up your custom voice slots.

If you delete a voice that you've saved from the [Voice Library](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-library) that is no longer shared, you will not be able to save it to [My Voices](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-lab) again in the future.

Unverified Professional Voice Clones cannot be deleted. Please [contact Support](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=13145996177937) if you need help with this.

If you have shared a voice with the Voice Library, you will need to stop sharing it before you can delete it. For details on how to do this, please see [How do I delete a voice I've shared with the Voice Library?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/27948860521745-How-do-I-delete-a-voice-I-ve-shared-with-the-Voice-Library)

content:
# How can I identify the voice used to generate audio? – ElevenLabs

You can use audio or video files to search the [Voice Library](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-library) for similar voices. The original voice used will be shown if it exists in the Voice Library, otherwise you will get suggestions for similar voices.

To do this, either drag and drop an audio or video file anywhere on the [Voice Library](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-library) page, or click the upload icon to select a file to upload.

![Image 29-10-2025 at 09.37.jpeg](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/40421117743121)

For the best results, please upload speech only, without background music or noise.  You can use our [Voice Isolator](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-isolator) to clean up the audio if required. 

Long clips are not required for the AI to find a match.

content:
# How do I add a custom preview for my shared voice? – ElevenLabs

You can generate a custom preview for your Professional Voice Clone, and choose this to be the preview for your voice in the [Voice Library](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-library). You can change the custom preview at any time from the sharing settings for your voice.

First, you will need to have generated some suitable audio from a text prompt between 70 and 150 characters long.

If you don't already have any eligible generations, find your voice in [My Voices](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-lab), and use the **T** button to open [Text to Speech](https://elevenlabs.io/app/speech-synthesis/text-to-speech) with your voice selected. Enter text of your choice between 70-150 characters and click **Generate speech.**

When you have a generation you're happy with, return to [My Voices.](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-lab) Find your voice in the list, click **More actions** (three dots) then select **Share voice** to access the sharing settings.

In the **Publish to the Voice Library** settings, use the drop down selection for **Custom voice preview** to select your recently generated text. This will then be applied as the preview for your voice. Only audio that meets the criteria (length of 70-150 characters) will appear in the list.

![Screenshot 2025-06-06 152445.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/35971482008209)

content:
# What is Audio Native? – ElevenLabs

Audio Native is an embedded audio player that automatically voices content of a web page using ElevenLab’s text-to-speech service. It can also be used to embed pre-generated content from a Studio project into a web page. All it takes to deploy on your site is a brief snippet of html. In addition, Audio Native comes with built-in metrics so you can track audience engagement through a listener dashboard.

Audio Native is available on the Creator plan and above.

For more details, please see our [Audio Native overview.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/creative-platform/audio-tools/audio-native)

content:
# What is usage based billing? – ElevenLabs

On the Creator plan and above, you have the option to activate usage based billing. This will enable you to pay for additional credits used after exceeding your monthly quota. For full details on how to enable this feature, please see [How do I enable usage based billing for my account?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/35899292474641-How-do-I-enable-usage-based-billing-for-my-account) 

To allow for more control over charges, usage based billing has a maximum number of credits that can be used. You can set this threshold yourself when you enable usage based billing. For a guide on how to do this, please see [How do I change the usage based billing threshold for my account?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/35899480663313-How-do-I-change-the-usage-based-billing-threshold-for-my-account)

The additional credits are either billed at the end of your billing cycle, or when the outstanding balance crosses the relevant threshold for your subscription:

*   Creator: $44
*   Pro: $198
*   Scale: $660
*   Business: $660

If you decide to upgrade or downgrade your subscription, you will be immediately charged for any outstanding usage based balance, as well as the price of your new chosen subscription plan.

Usage based billing offers a fixed price for each additional 1,000 credits used over your limit.  The cost of these credits depends on your plan. For details, please see [What is the price for usage based billing?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/24351324241937-What-is-the-price-for-usage-based-billing)

content:
# How does credit rollover work? – ElevenLabs

Credit rollover allows you to carry over up to twice your usual credit quota when you begin a new billing cycle, provided you remain on the same subscription.

On our standard subscription plans (Starter, Creator, Pro, Scale and Business), your credits will reset at the beginning of each billing cycle. If you have unused credits at the end of your billing cycle, you will automatically rollover up to twice your usual monthly quota.

Please note that credit rollover does not apply to accounts on the free tier. If you cancel or downgrade your subscription, this will take effect at the end of your current billing cycle, and you will lose any unused credits on your account when this happens.

On our Enterprise plans, you can decide whether you want to receive your usage quota on a monthly or annual basis. If you have unused usage quota at the end of the month or year, depending on your choice, you will automatically rollover up to twice your usual quota.

This means that the maximum number of your usage quota you can have at any one time is your subscription's usual quota multiplied by 3.

For example, on the Scale plan, the maximum number of credits you can have is 2,000,000 x 3 = 6,000,000.

If you upgrade to an annual plan while you have an active monthly subscription, this will start a new annual billing cycle. All unused credits from your previous character quota will be added to the first month's quota of your new annual plan.

If you downgrade or cancel your annual subscription, this does not take effect until the end of your current annual billing cycle, and you will lose any unused credits on your account when this happens.

content:
# What are credits? – ElevenLabs

Credits were previously referred to as "characters." We updated this terminology to streamline pricing and make it easier to understand, though the value remains unchanged.

Credits can be used to generate and transform audio across our platform. The number of credits used depends on your plan, the product you’re using, and the model involved.

**Flash Models (Flash v2 and Flash v2.5) and Turbo Models (Turbo v2 and Turbo v2.5)**

*   For **self-serve plans**, one text character equals 0.5 credits.
*   For **enterprise plans**, one text character equals between 0.5 and 1 credit (to account for existing enterprise discounts).

**Other Models (English V1, Multilingual V1, and Multilingual V2)** 

*   One text character equals 1 credit for all customers.

Please note that some shared voices in our [Voice Library](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/23143350045329-What-is-the-Voice-Library) have [custom rates](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/26050485079057-What-are-custom-rates) that apply a multiplier to your credit consumption.

Audio credits are calculated per minute, considering factors like custom voices, the Voice Isolator tool, SFX, and the Dubbing Studio.

**Credit Allocation and Rollover**

For self-serve monthly and annual plans, and Enterprise monthly plans: you will receive a set amount of credits each month, which reset monthly. Up to two months’ worth of unused credits can roll over to the next month.

For enterprise annual plans, you will receive a yearly credit allocation upfront. These credits reset each year, with up to two months' worth of unused credits rolling over to the next year.

If you downgrade or cancel your subscription, this does not take effect until the end of your current billing cycle, and unused credits are not rolled over.

metadata.description: Credits were previously referred to as "characters." We updated this terminology to streamline pricing and make it easier to...

content:
# Have characters changed? – ElevenLabs

Credits were previously referred to as "characters." We updated this terminology to streamline pricing and make it easier to understand, though the value remains unchanged.

Previously, this terminology was confusing, as “characters” were also used for tools that had non-text-related inputs like speech-to-speech and dubbing. We think "credits" is a more appropriate name as they can be used across all of the tools in our product.

Regarding text-to-speech, you will still see the terminology "characters" as this refers to written text characters. For our English V1, Multilingual V1, and Multilingual V2 models, each text "character" you generate into speech will consume 1 credit. For our Turbo v2 and v2.5 models, each text "character" will consume 0.5 credits, unless you have a custom enterprise plan. Note that some shared voices in our Voice Library have a custom rate that applies a multiplier to your credit consumption.

content:
# How do I change my email? – ElevenLabs

If you signed up using an email and password, you can change your email from your profile: 

1.  Click **My Account** in the bottom-left corner of the website.
2.  Select **Settings.**
3.  Click **Change Email.**
4.  Enter your new email address.

An email will be sent to both your current email address and your new email address. To complete the process, click the verification links to confirm the change.

**Important:**

You can't change your email if an account already uses the new address. The new email must be completely unused by any ElevenLabs accounts.

If you signed up using Google or Apple Sign-in, you cannot change your email, as your account is intrinsically linked to your Google or Apple account.

content:
# How can I find out what voice is used in a video or audio clip? – ElevenLabs

You can usually hear our technology used all across the internet on social media, on YouTube, in marketing material, and sometimes you might want to know what voice is being used in this material. Luckily, we offer a tool just for this.

If you go to the [Voice Library](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-library) and look to the right of the search bar, you will see a little button that allows you to upload a file to use as the search method. Click this button and upload the file you want to search.

![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/27791992237457)

The AI will then present you with the most similar voices or the exact matches if there is one.

It's important to keep in mind when searching that you should only include the audio of the spoken voice and nothing else. If there's any background noise, music, or anything else in the audio clip, you should remove it and only include the actual audio of the voice. If you don't have any clean audio, you can use the [Voice Isolator](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-isolator) first to isolate the voice and then upload that to get a more accurate match.

Also, please keep in mind that this will only search the voices available in the public voice library and not any custom voices from other sources.

openGraph.content: ElevenLabs was founded in 2022 and focuses primarily on advanced generative AI for audio. The company was co-founded by Piotr Dąbkowski, a former Google machine learning engineer, and Mateusz Stani...

content:
# What is ElevenLabs? – ElevenLabs

ElevenLabs was founded in 2022 and focuses primarily on advanced generative AI for audio. The company was co-founded by Piotr Dąbkowski, a former Google machine learning engineer, and Mateusz Staniszewski, an ex-Palantir strategist.

The motivation to start ElevenLabs stemmed from a desire to improve the quality of voice dubbing in films, which led them to develop the backbone for their text-to-speech (TTS) solutions.

Their mission is to make content accessible in any language and in any voice.

Currently, ElevenLabs offer an extensive array of tools and technologies:

*   [Text to speech](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/capabilities/text-to-speech)
*   [Voice changer](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/capabilities/voice-changer)
*   [Voice cloning and voice design](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/capabilities/voices)
*   [Dubbing](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/capabilities/dubbing)
*   [Sound effects](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product/sound-effects/overview)
*   And much more!

At ElevenLabs, we are constantly trying to innovate and push the bounds of what is possible with generative AI.

To learn more, we urge you to check out our [beautiful landing pages](https://elevenlabs.io/), as well as read through our [extensive documentation](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/) to discover what's possible.

metadata.description: To remove a voice that you have shared with the Voice Library, you first need to stop sharing the voice.  To do this, find the voice in...

openGraph.content: How do I delete a voice I've shared with the Voice Library?

To remove a voice that you have shared with the Voice Library, you first need to stop sharing the voice.  To do this, find the voice in the list of voices in My Voices, click More actions (three do...

content:
# How do I delete a voice I've shared with the Voice Library? – ElevenLabs

To remove a voice that you have shared with the Voice Library, you first need to stop sharing the voice.  To do this, find the voice in the list of voices in [My Voices](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-lab), click **More actions** (three dots) then select **Share voice** to access the sharing settings.

![Screenshot 2025-06-06 110034.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/35971119591313)

Click the **Publish to the Voice Library** toggle to stop sharing your voice with the Voice Library.

If you did not agree a notice period when you shared the voice, you can delete the voice after you have disabled sharing. 

To do this, click **More actions** (three dots) then select **Delete voice**. Deleting a voice cannot be undone and you will be asked to confirm the deletion.

If you did agree a notice period, you will need to wait for this to expire before you can remove your voice. During the notice period, your voice can be used by anyone who had already saved it to My Voices, but it will no longer be visible in the Voice Library. 

Once you have activated the notice period, you will see a clock icon which you can hover over for confirmation.  Please be aware that if you share your voice with the Voice Library again in the future, by enabling the **Discovery in Voice Library** toggle, this will reset your notice period.

metadata.title: What does the error 'No model found for this voice. Please select another voice' mean? – ElevenLabs

openGraph.content: What does the error 'No model found for this voice. Please select another voice' mean?

You will see this error if you try to use your Professional Voice Clone (PVC) before it has completed the fine-tuning process and is available for use.When you create a PVC, it needs to go through ...

content:
# What does the error 'No model found for this voice. Please select another voice' mean? – ElevenLabs

You will see this error if you try to use your Professional Voice Clone (PVC) before it has completed the fine-tuning process and is available for use.

When you create a PVC, it needs to go through a number of processes before it becomes available for use.  After you have verified your voice, it will be processed and queued for fine-tuning.   Depending on how many other voices are currently queued for fine-tuning, we estimate that this process will usually take between 3-6, but it can take up to 24 hours.

You can check the progress of your PVC in My Voices by finding the voice in your list of voices, then clicking **View** to see more details.  You can hover over each model to see the current status.  

![Screenshot 2024-09-02 104204.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/28006635136785)

For more detail on what each status means, please see [What does the status of my Professional Voice Clone mean?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/24868008818833-What-does-the-status-of-my-Professional-Voice-Clone-mean)

When your PVC has completed fine-tuning and is available for use, you will see a pop-up notification, and will also be notified by email.

metadata.title: How can I keep the WebSocket open? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: If you're working with the text-to-speech WebSockets endpoint via our API, the connection will automatically close after 20 seconds of...

openGraph.content: How can I keep the WebSocket open?

If you're working with the text-to-speech WebSockets endpoint via our API, the connection will automatically close after 20 seconds of inactivity.
To keep the connection open, you can send a single...

content:
# How can I keep the WebSocket open? – ElevenLabs

If you're working with the text-to-speech WebSockets endpoint via our API, the connection will automatically close after 20 seconds of inactivity.

To keep the connection open, you can send a single space character " ". Please note that this string must include a space, as sending a fully empty string, "", will send an End of Sequence (EOS) message, closing the socket.

This will keep the connection open for another 20 seconds.  To keep the connection open for longer, you will need to send additional single space characters " " every 20 seconds.  

You can also specify how long the connection can be inactive before it's automatically closed.  The default value is 20 seconds, but you can change this by adding `inactivity_timeout` as a query parameter in the WebSockets endpoint.  The maximum allowed value is 180 seconds.

For more information, please see the [WebSockets API Reference.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/websockets)

metadata.title: What is the Text-to-Speech WebSockets API? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: The WebSocket streaming endpoint, also sometimes referred to as input streaming, provides real-time text-to-speech conversion using...

openGraph.content: What is the Text-to-Speech WebSockets API?

The WebSocket streaming endpoint, also sometimes referred to as input streaming, provides real-time text-to-speech conversion using WebSockets. This allows you to send a text message and receive au...

content:
# What is the Text-to-Speech WebSockets API? – ElevenLabs

The WebSocket streaming endpoint, also sometimes referred to as input streaming, provides real-time [text-to-speech](https://elevenlabs.io/text-to-speech) conversion using WebSockets. This allows you to send a text message and receive audio data back in real-time.

It is designed to start generating audio chunks from just partial text chunks as input. It can help minimize latency by starting to generate the output before the whole context is delivered and is supposed to be used in conjunction with other tools such as Large Language Models (LLMs). The generated audio will still sound like a string of continuous speech.

Although highly flexible, the WebSockets API isn’t a one-size-fits-all solution.

It’s well-suited for scenarios where:

*   The input text is being streamed or generated in chunks.
*   The lowest possible latency is needed.
*   Word-to-audio alignment information is required.

 It may not be the best choice when:

*   The entire input text is delivered at once. Given that the generations are partial, some buffering is involved, which could potentially result in slightly higher latency compared to a standard HTTP request. Instead, using only the output streaming endpoint is most likely the better option in cases like this.
    
*   You want to quickly experiment or prototype. Working with WebSockets can be harder and more complex than using a standard HTTP API, which might slow down rapid development and testing.
*   Where the highest possible consistency is of utmost importance, you need to consider the AI's context understanding. Since the AI is not given the full context at the beginning of the request, it will only have a partial understanding of the text, which in certain instances can cause issues. However, you can experiment with some of the settings available when using WebSocket streaming, such as the "chunk\_length\_schedule". This parameter will determine how big the chunks need to be before the AI starts generating text.
    

In these cases, use the [Text to Speech API](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/text-to-speech) instead.

To find out more, please see the [WebSockets API Reference.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/websockets)

content:
# How much does it cost to use the API? – ElevenLabs

The API is included in all plans, even the free plan, and there is no extra cost associated with the API except for the cost of the actual generation. This means that if you send a request to the API using your API key, it will deduct credits from your account, just as if you were using the service via the website.

You can find the exact credit pricing [here](https://elevenlabs.io/pricing).

content:
# How do I use my coupon link? – ElevenLabs

Coupon links can only be used if you are currently on the free plan.  Please [get in touch with Support](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=13145996177937) if you are trying to use a coupon link but are currently on a paid subscription.

1.  Create a free account, or log into your free account.

2.  Click the coupon link.

3.  The coupon link will activate and be automatically applied. You will see a notification confirming this.

4.  Click **My Account** in the bottom left corner of the website then select **[Subscription](https://elevenlabs.io/app/subscription)**.      

5.  Select **Subscribe** underneath the plan that your coupon is valid for.

6.  The discount will be applied at checkout. You will see confirmation that it has been applied successfully before you complete checkout.

content:
# Are there any limitations to the size of a project in Studio? – ElevenLabs

In general, most people won't reach these limits, but there are a few limitations that are good to keep in mind.

*   Up to 500 chapters per project
*   Each chapter can have up to 400 paragraphs
*   Each paragraph can have a maximum of 5000 characters

Additionally, each subscription plan includes a limit on the total number of projects you can have. If you exceed this limit prior to downgrading or canceling your plan, you will retain access to your existing projects. However, you will not be able to create new projects until the total number falls within the limits of your current plan.  
**  
Project Limits by Plan:**

*   **Free:** 5 projects
    
*   **Starter:** 20 projects
    
*   **Creator:** 1,000 projects
    
*   **Pro:** 3,000 projects
    
*   **Scale:** 20,000 projects
    
*   **Business:** 20,000 projects

If you're using Chrome on Windows, please try these steps to make sure your microphone is working correctly on ElevenLabs.
1. In Windows Privacy and Security menu, select Microphone Privacy Setting...

content:
# How can I make sure my microphone is working? – ElevenLabs

If you're using Chrome on Windows, please try these steps to make sure your microphone is working correctly on ElevenLabs.

1\. In Windows Privacy and Security menu, select Microphone Privacy Settings, then enable Microphone access.

![Screenshot 2024-10-08 170748.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/29040322052497)

2\. In Chrome, reset the permissions and set them again to make sure that the website has permission to use the microphone.  Click the arrow next to Microphone, then click the toggle to enable it, and select 'Allow on every visit'.  Make sure it says 'Allowed'. 

![Screenshot 2024-10-08 171538-001.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/29040322055313)   ![Screenshot 2024-10-08 172359.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/29040322056081)

3\. In Chrome, go to chrome://settings/content/microphone or select 'Settings' in your menu, then select 'Privacy and security', then under 'Permissions', select 'Microphone'.  Make sure that the correct microphone is selected in the menu.

4\. You can test that your microphone is working by using [Voice Changer](https://elevenlabs.io/app/speech-synthesis/speech-to-speech). 

![Screenshot 2024-10-08 173525.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/29040322057361)

Click 'Record Audio', then click the microphone icon to begin recording.  Record a quick test, then press the stop icon to stop recording.  You can then play back your recording to check that the microphone is working correctly.  You will not be charged unless you click 'Generate speech'.

metadata.title: Can I upgrade my annual subscription? – ElevenLabs

openGraph.content: Can I upgrade my annual subscription?

You can upgrade from one annual subscription to another annual subscription, but you can't upgrade from an annual subscription to a monthly subscription.

content:
# Can I upgrade my annual subscription? – ElevenLabs

You can upgrade from one annual subscription to another annual subscription, but you can't upgrade from an annual subscription to a monthly subscription.

When upgrading your annual subscription, here's what happens:

*   If you have enabled usage based billing, and incurred a charge, this will be billed when you upgrade.
*   You will receive a prorated discount on your new plan. This discount is based on the unused months of your current plan, minus the two free months included in the annual subscription price.
*   You will be charged the full cost of your new annual plan, which also includes two free months, minus the prorated discount from your previous plan.
*   Your new annual subscription will begin immediately, resetting your billing cycle. It will automatically renew a year later unless canceled.  If you do cancel, the cancelation will take effect at the end of your current billing cycle.

content:
# How do I make a Data Subject Request? – ElevenLabs

To access your data, you can log in to your account on [ElevenLabs.io](https://elevenlabs.io/) and navigate to the History tab to find all of your generated data.

To correct, edit, or delete specific data, where applicable, you can do so directly on the platform.

To delete your account, you can do so via the platform by following the steps in this guide: [How can I delete my account?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/13313906314641-How-can-I-delete-my-account)

To open a formal Data Subject Request, please use the following link. This is the only official way to open such a request: [Submit a Data Subject Request](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=25300656511761).

Please also see our [Terms of Service](https://elevenlabs.io/terms-of-use) and [Privacy Policy](https://elevenlabs.io/privacy-policy) for more information about how we treat your personal data.

content:
# What is ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI)? – ElevenLabs

ElevenLabs Agents is a platform for deploying customized, interactive voice agents. Our ElevenLabs Agents orchestration platform combines

*   Speech to Text (the ears)
*   LLM (the brain)
*   Text to Speech (the voice)

along with built in interruption handling, turn taking logic, and knowledge bases. Altogether, our platform makes it easy to create interactive agents that you and your users can talk to like a person.

For more information on ElevenLabs Agents, please see our [documentation](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/overview). 

We also have a series of YouTube videos and tutorials covering our Agents platform. You can visit [our YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@elevenlabsio) to access these.

metadata.description: You can create fully customized and interactive voice agents.  You can customize the voice, the first message and the system prompt for...

openGraph.content: What can I create with ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI)

You can create fully customized and interactive voice agents. 
You can customize the voice, the first message and the system prompt for your agent. The system prompt will inform your agent’s behavi...

content:
# What can I create with ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI) – ElevenLabs

You can create fully customized and interactive voice agents. 

You can customize the voice, the first message and the system prompt for your agent. The system prompt will inform your agent’s behavior, style, and demeanor.  

You can also choose the LLM your agent uses. We offer ElevenLabs hosted LLMs for ultra-low latency and reduced reasoning cost. You can also chose from the leading models from Anthropic, OpenAI and Google, or use your own custom LLM.

You can also create your own knowledge base. This is additional information you can provide your agent on top of the base understanding of the LLM you selected. Non-enterprise users are limited up to 20MB in total. 

RAG allows you to add more information to your agent's knowledge base. RAG cannot be enabled for documents smaller than 500 bytes. When RAG is enabled, the size of knowledge base items that can be assigned to an agent is increased. The total limit is dependent on your subscription plan - see our [Agents documentation](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/customization/knowledge-base/rag#usage-limits) for details. 

For more information on how to start creating your voice agent, please see our [ElevenLabs Agents documentation.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/overview)

metadata.title: What is Voice Design? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: Voice Design lets you create a unique voice from a text prompt.It’s designed to help when you can’t find the exact voice you need in our...

openGraph.content: Voice Design lets you create a unique voice from a text prompt.It’s designed to help when you can’t find the exact voice you need in our Voice Library. Instead of choosing from existing options, yo...

content:
# What is Voice Design? – ElevenLabs

Voice Design lets you create a unique voice from a text prompt.

It’s designed to help when you can’t find the exact voice you need in our Voice Library. Instead of choosing from existing options, you can now generate a custom voice by describing it in your own words.

To get started, just type a prompt describing the voice you want. You can include details like accent, gender, pacing, tone, and speaking style. The more specific your prompt, the more closely the voice will match your intent. But if you're unsure, a simple prompt like "a calm male narrator" also works.

Voices created with Voice Design v3 are fully compatible with our latest model, v3 (Alpha), and support audio tags for our most expressive speech. They’re also backward compatible, meaning you can use them across all our other models.

For more tips on writing effective prompts, see our [Voice Design guide.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/creative-platform/voices/voice-design)

**Note:** Voice Design is still experimental. Professional Voice Clones offer the highest quality and consistency. If you find a PVC that fits your needs, we recommend using it.

There is no cost to create your Conversational AI agent, and calls made during setup and prompt testing are billed at half the cost. The cost of calls depends on whether your agent is voice only, m...

content:
# How much does ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI) cost? – ElevenLabs

There is no cost to create your Conversational AI agent, and calls made during setup and prompt testing are billed at half the cost. 

The cost of calls depends on whether your agent is voice only, multimodal or text only. 

**Voice only**

Voice only calls are charged based on the call duration, with a 95% discount for periods of silence longer than 10 seconds. LLM costs are passed through separately - for estimates, see [LLM costs](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/customization/llm#supported-llms). 

|     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Tier** | **Price** | **Minutes included** | **Cost in credits per extra minute** | **Cost in credits per $1 LLM cost** |
| Free | $0  | 15  | Unavailable | 10,000 |
| Starter | $5  | 50  | Unavailable | 6,000 |
| Creator | $22 | 250 | 400 | 4,545 |
| Pro | $99 | 1,100 | 454 | 5,051 |
| Scale | $330 | 3,600 | 555 | 6,061 |
| Business | $1,320 | 13,750 | 800 | 8,333 |

Usage based billing needs to be enabled for your account before you can exceed your credit quota. You can find out more about this here: [What is usage based billing?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/27378406011409-What-is-usage-based-billing)

The length of the call is measured based on the connection duration. This includes the time from when you begin the call, to when you end the call or the window is closed. This is why the call itself may be shorter than the duration you are charged for.

**Multimodal**

Multimodal calls are charged based on the call duration, with a 95% discount for periods of silence longer than 10 seconds. You're also charged for each text message - for costs, see the table below. LLM costs are passed through separately - for estimates, see [LLM costs](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/customization/llm#supported-llms). 

**Text only**

In text only calls you're charged for each text message - for costs, see the table below. LLM costs are passed through separately - for estimates, see [LLM costs](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/customization/llm#supported-llms). 

**Cost of text messages - this will be converted to credits for self service subscription plans.**

|     |     |
| --- | --- | 
| Plan | Price per text message |
| Free | 0.4 cents |
| Starter | 0.4 cents |
| Creator | 0.3 cents |
| Pro | 0.3 cents |
| Scale | 0.3 cents |
| Business | 0.3 cents |

For more information on how to start creating your voice agent, please see our [ElevenLabs Agents documentation.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/overview)

# How much does Voice Design cost? – ElevenLabs

Voice Design only consumes credits when you generate voices.

Each time you click **Generate voice**, we create three voice options based on your prompt. You're only charged for the number of characters in your preview text.

You can enter your own preview text or use the **Auto-generate** button to create one automatically. Auto-generated previews include relevant audio tags.

For more information, see our [Voice Design guide.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product-guides/voices/voice-design)

content:
# What kind of voices can I create using Voice Design? – ElevenLabs

Voice Design can generate a wide range of voices, from realistic, human-like voices to more creative, character-style voices.

If you're unsure where to start, try a simple prompt like "middle-aged female newsreader." However, more detailed prompts typically produce more accurate and nuanced results.

You can include a variety of characteristics in your prompt, such as:

*   age
    
*   gender
    
*   accent
    
*   tone
    
*   pacing
    
*   emotion
    
*   speaking style
    
*   audio quality
    

Two additional controls help shape the output:

*   **Loudness** adjusts the volume of both the preview and the saved voice.
    
*   **Guidance scale** determines how closely the generated voice follows your prompt.
    

For more help crafting prompts, see our [Voice Design guide](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product/voices/voice-lab/voice-design).

content:
# Can I add users to my account? – ElevenLabs

Currently, the only plans that support multi-seat Workspaces are the Scale, Business and Enterprise plans. It is not possible to add additional users to any of our other subscription plans.

On the Scale plan, each Workspace comes with 3 seats - one admin user and two additional users.

On the Business plan, each Workspace comes with 5 seats - one admin user and four additional users.

For more information, please see our [Workspaces documentation](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product-guides/administration/workspaces/overview).

content:
# Is my data used to improve ElevenLabs’ AI models? – ElevenLabs

ElevenLabs uses certain data you provide to us to improve the quality of our audio models for everyone. We don’t use your data to clone your voice without your consent or create advertising profiles of you.

By default, we don’t train on any data from our Enterprise customers, other than as may be necessary to provide services to such Enterprise customers. Anyone can opt out of data use at any time by following the instructions below.

**How can I modify my data use settings?**

You can modify your data use settings at any time by clicking your profile icon in the bottom-left of any page in the ElevenLabs platform and navigating to “Terms and privacy” >  “Data use.”

![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/29952698707857)

In the ‘Data use’ menu, you’ll see a toggle called ‘Improve the models for everyone.’ To stop sharing your data with us, disable the toggle as shown in the image below and click ‘Update your choice.’ Once the toggle is disabled, new data you submit to us won’t be used to train our models.

![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/29952698710161)

Note that if you previously submitted an opt-out request to us via Typeform, your choice should be reflected in your Data use dialog. For more information, please see our [Privacy Policy](https://elevenlabs.io/privacy-policy).

# Your ticket has been verified! – ElevenLabs

If you see this page, it most likely means that you were redirected here because you've verified your ticket.

So, now that your ticket has been verified, you should have received a verification email informing you of this, along with any further instructions if applicable.

In most cases, all you have to do now is wait. The team will address your inquiry as soon as possible.

content:
# What is Generation History in Studio? – ElevenLabs

Generation History allows you to listen to, download and restore previous audio generations for each paragraph.  

![studio-generation-history.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41239431499409)

The Generation History for each paragraph appears in the left sidebar when you select it. You can see and listen to all the previous generations for the selected paragraph. 

If you prefer an earlier generation, you can restore this by clicking **Restore previous generation**. This audio will then appear as the current version of the paragraph in your project. You can use the lock button to lock the paragraph to prevent further changes. 

For each generation, you can download the individual audio file. To download, click **More actions** (three dots) > **Download**. 

You can also remove generations, but please bear in mind that removing a generation is permanent, and it cannot be recovered. To remove a generation, click **More actions** (three dots) > **Remove**. 

![studio-generation-history-detail.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41239431499921)

content:
# What does the grey line mean in Studio? – ElevenLabs

In Studio, the grey line to the left of a paragraph indicates the audio generation status. A light grey line signifies that audio has not yet been generated, while a dark grey line indicates that audio has been successfully generated.

In the image below, the highlighted paragraph has audio generated, whereas the second one does not.

![studio-generated-light.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41239023143953)

In Dark Mode, this color scheme adjusts slightly: a light grey line still represents ungenerated audio, while a white line indicates that the paragraph has been converted to audio.

The image below demonstrates this: audio has been generated for the highlighted paragraph, but not for the second one.

![studio-generated-dark.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41238992402449)

content:
# Can I regenerate individual words in Studio? – ElevenLabs

In Studio, you can either regenerate the whole of the selected paragraph, or if you select one or more words, you can regenerate only those words. For the best results, we recommend you regenerate a complete phrase or sentence at a time.

You need to have generated audio for the whole paragraph before you will see the option to regenerate selection.

![Screenshot 2025-11-24 at 17.09.53.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41238632739601)

# Create audio native project | ElevenLabs Documentation Creates Audio Native enabled project, optionally starts conversion and returns project ID and embeddable HTML snippet. ### Request This endpoint expects a multipart form containing an optional file. namestringRequired Project name. imagestring or nullOptionalDeprecated (Deprecated) Image URL used in the player. If not provided, default image set in the Player settings is used. authorstring or nullOptional Author used in the player and inserted at the start of the uploaded article. If not provided, the default author set in the Player settings is used. titlestring or nullOptional Title used in the player and inserted at the top of the uploaded article. If not provided, the default title set in the Player settings is used. smallbooleanOptionalDefaults to `false`Deprecated (Deprecated) Whether to use small player or not. If not provided, default value set in the Player settings is used. text\_colorstring or nullOptional Text color used in the player. If not provided, default text color set in the Player settings is used. background\_colorstring or nullOptional Background color used in the player. If not provided, default background color set in the Player settings is used. sessionizationintegerOptionalDefaults to `0`Deprecated (Deprecated) Specifies for how many minutes to persist the session across page reloads. If not provided, default sessionization set in the Player settings is used. voice\_idstring or nullOptional Voice ID used to voice the content. If not provided, default voice ID set in the Player settings is used. model\_idstring or nullOptional TTS Model ID used in the player. If not provided, default model ID set in the Player settings is used. filefileOptional Either txt or HTML input file containing the article content. HTML should be formatted as follows ‘

Your content

More of your content

Some more of your content

’ auto\_convertbooleanOptionalDefaults to `false` Whether to auto convert the project to audio or not. apply\_text\_normalizationenum or nullOptional This parameter controls text normalization with four modes: ‘auto’, ‘on’, ‘apply\_english’ and ‘off’. When set to ‘auto’, the system will automatically decide whether to apply text normalization (e.g., spelling out numbers). With ‘on’, text normalization will always be applied, while with ‘off’, it will be skipped. ‘apply\_english’ is the same as ‘on’ but will assume that text is in English. Allowed values: pronunciation\_dictionary\_locatorslist of stringsOptional A list of pronunciation dictionary locators (pronunciation\_dictionary\_id, version\_id) encoded as a list of JSON strings for pronunciation dictionaries to be applied to the text. A list of json encoded strings is required as adding projects may occur through formData as opposed to jsonBody. To specify multiple dictionaries use multiple —form lines in your curl, such as —form ‘pronunciation\_dictionary\_locators=”{“pronunciation\_dictionary\_id”:“Vmd4Zor6fplcA7WrINey”,“version\_id”:“hRPaxjlTdR7wFMhV4w0b”}”’ —form ‘pronunciation\_dictionary\_locators=”{“pronunciation\_dictionary\_id”:“JzWtcGQMJ6bnlWwyMo7e”,“version\_id”:“lbmwxiLu4q6txYxgdZqn”}”’. ### Response Successful Response project\_idstring The ID of the created Audio Native project. convertingboolean Whether the project is currently being converted. html\_snippetstring The HTML snippet to embed the Audio Native player.

content:
# What does the lock icon mean in Studio? – ElevenLabs

In Studio, you can lock a paragraph once you're happy with it, and this will prevent any accidental changes. Locked paragraphs are indicated by a lock icon to the left of the paragraph. 

The lock button locks the current version of the selected paragraph. This means that you no longer have the option to regenerate the paragraph, change the voice or settings, or access the generation history for the paragraph. You can unlock a paragraph at any time by clicking the lock button.

![studio-lock.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41238554053521)

This feature is only available after you have generated audio for the selected paragraph.

content:
# Can Children’s or Child-Like Voices Be Added to the Voice Library? – ElevenLabs

No, children’s voices or voices that sound child-like cannot be added to the Library. This policy applies to voices of minors as well as adult voices designed to mimic or sound like children.

We made this decision to align with our [Prohibited Use Policy](https://elevenlabs.io/use-policy), which prioritizes safeguarding against any potential misuse of voices that could pose risks to children. Voices resembling children could be misused in harmful or exploitative ways, and this restriction helps ensure that our platform remains a safe and responsible space for creators and users alike.

For more information, please refer to our [Prohibited Use Policy.](https://elevenlabs.io/use-policy)

If you have further questions or need clarification, feel free to [contact our support team.](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=13145996177937)

metadata.description: If you make changes to your project, you need to export a new version of your chapter or project. This will save the updated audio to a...

If you make changes to your project, you need to export a new version of your chapter or project. This will save the updated audio to a new version so that it is reflected in your download. To do t...

content:
# Why aren't my changes reflected in my download from Studio? – ElevenLabs

If you make changes to your project, you need to export a new version of your chapter or project. This will save the updated audio to a new version so that it is reflected in your download. To do this, click **Export**, and choose your preferred options.

If you choose to export your full project, Studio will automatically convert any paragraphs that are not yet converted throughout all chapters in your project, which will cost credits. If this is the case, you will be notified of how many credits it will cost before you confirm the export.

![Screenshot 2025-11-19 at 16.31.19.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41238430698385)

If you have already generated audio for all paragraphs, there will be no cost, but the audio still needs to be exported to a new version before it is reflected in your download. You will be notified when the conversion is complete and your download is ready.

You can access and download previous exports by clicking the **Export** button, and selecting the **History** tab.

content:
# Can I assign more than one voice to a paragraph in Studio? – ElevenLabs

You can assign multiple voices to a single paragraph in Studio. To do this, you just need to select the text you want to assign to each voice, and change the voice using the voice selection drop-down in the Voices sidebar, on the left of the screen.  

Each voice will be designated with a different color icon to the left of the paragraph, and the text associated with each voice will be highlighted in a corresponding color.

![Screenshot 2025-11-24 at 16.45.42.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41237759485201)

metadata.title: Can I share an AI-generated voice in the Voice Library? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: No. We only allow the sharing of professional cloned voices verified by a human. Instant Voice Clones, synthetic voices created using...

openGraph.content: Can I share an AI-generated voice in the Voice Library?

No. We only allow the sharing of professional cloned voices verified by a human. Instant Voice Clones, synthetic voices created using Voice Design, AI-generated or AI-enhanced voices cannot be shar...

content:
# Can I share an AI-generated voice in the Voice Library? – ElevenLabs

No. We only allow the sharing of professional cloned voices verified by a human. Instant Voice Clones, synthetic voices created using [Voice Design](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product/voices/voice-lab/voice-design), AI-generated or AI-enhanced voices cannot be shared in the Voice Library.

Users attempting to share voices generated with ElevenLabs or other AI tools into the library might be prohibited from sharing any voices in the future or suspended from the platform.

Engaging in fraudulent, predatory or abusive practices (like evading product guardrails including voice verification (e.g., CAPTCHA) is against our “Prohibited Use Policy” and might lead to a permanent ban of the user’s account.

content:
# Developer quickstart | ElevenLabs Documentation

The ElevenLabs API provides a simple interface to state-of-the-art audio [models](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/overview/models) and [features](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/introduction). Follow this guide to learn how to create lifelike speech with our Text to Speech API. See the [developer guides](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/developers/quickstart#explore-our-developer-guides) for more examples with our other products.

## Using the Text to Speech API

[1](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/developers/quickstart#create-an-api-key)

### Create an API key

[Create an API key in the dashboard here](https://elevenlabs.io/app/settings/api-keys), which you’ll use to securely [access the API](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/authentication).

Store the key as a managed secret and pass it to the SDKs either as a environment variable via an `.env` file, or directly in your app’s configuration depending on your preference.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1   | ELEVENLABS\_API\_KEY=<your\_api\_key\_here> |

[2](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/developers/quickstart#install-the-sdk)

### Install the SDK

We’ll also use the `dotenv` library to load our API key from an environment variable.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1   | pip install elevenlabs |
| 2   | pip install python-dotenv |

To play the audio through your speakers, you may be prompted to install [MPV](https://mpv.io/) and/or [ffmpeg](https://ffmpeg.org/).

[3](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/developers/quickstart#make-your-first-request)

### Make your first request

Create a new file named `example.py` or `example.mts`, depending on your language of choice and add the following code:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1   | from dotenv import load\_dotenv |
| 2   | from elevenlabs.client import ElevenLabs |
| 3   | from elevenlabs.play import play |
| 4   | import os |
| 5   |     |
| 6   | load\_dotenv() |
| 7   |     |
| 8   | elevenlabs = ElevenLabs( |
| 9   | api\_key=os.getenv("ELEVENLABS\_API\_KEY"), |
| 10  | )   |
| 11  |     |
| 12  | audio = elevenlabs.text\_to\_speech.convert( |
| 13  | text="The first move is what sets everything in motion.", |
| 14  | voice\_id="JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", |
| 15  | model\_id="eleven\_multilingual\_v2", |
| 16  | output\_format="mp3\_44100\_128", |
| 17  | )   |
| 18  |     |
| 19  | play(audio) |

[4](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/developers/quickstart#run-the-code)

### Run the code

You should hear the audio play through your speakers.

## Explore our developer guides

Now that you’ve made your first ElevenLabs API request, you can explore the other products that ElevenLabs offers.

metadata.description: Auto-Regenerate is a feature in Studio that automatically checks your generated audio for any mispronunciations or unwanted audio...

openGraph.content: Auto-Regenerate is a feature in Studio that automatically checks your generated audio for any mispronunciations or unwanted audio artefacts. If we detect any, we will automatically regenerate the a...

content:
# What is Auto-Regenerate? – ElevenLabs

Auto-Regenerate is a feature in Studio that automatically checks your generated audio for any mispronunciations or unwanted audio artefacts. If we detect any, we will automatically regenerate the audio up to two times, at no extra cost.  

This feature is enabled automatically, and it is done in the backend when you convert your whole chapter or project in one step from the Export dialog.

![studio-export.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41237575574161)

content:
# Does it cost credits to regenerate in Studio? – ElevenLabs

We offer up to two free regenerations in Studio, provided you haven't changed the text that you're regenerating, or assigned a different voice, or changed the voice settings.

To regenerate, either click on the paragraph to regenerate the whole paragraph, or select one or more words that you want to regenerate. For the best results, we recommend regenerating a complete phrase or sentence. 

If the selection is eligible for a free regeneration, you will see that the Generate/Regenerate button in the toolbar says **Regenerate**, and if you hover over it, you will see a notification letting you know how many free regenerations are remaining.  

![Screenshot 2025-11-19 at 17.43.09.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41237476632977)

If your selection isn't eligible for a free regeneration, the button will say **Generate**, and you will be charged for the generation.

content:
# How do I download generated files from Voice Changer? – ElevenLabs

You can download the generated files in two ways:

You can download a generated file immediately by clicking the download button on the bottom right after generating the content.

Previously generated files can be downloaded from your history. To access your history, log in to your account and select **Voice Changer** in the sidebar, then click the **History** tab in the panel on the right side of the screen. 

You can click the download button to download any file from your history as either an MP3 or WAV file.  For additional download options, click **Advanced**.

openGraph.content: Converts text into speech using a voice of your choice and returns audio.

headers.content-security-policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' https: blob:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https:; img-src 'self' https: data: blob:; font-src 'self' https: data:; connect-src 'self' https: wss: ws: data: blob:; media-src 'self' https: data: blob:; object-src 'self' https: data: blob:; frame-src 'self' https: data: blob:; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self' https:

content:
# Create speech | ElevenLabs Documentation

Converts text into speech using a voice of your choice and returns audio.

### Path parameters

voice\_idstringRequired

ID of the voice to be used. Use the [Get voices](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/voices/search) endpoint list all the available voices.

### Query parameters

enable\_loggingbooleanOptionalDefaults to `true`

When enable\_logging is set to false zero retention mode will be used for the request. This will mean history features are unavailable for this request, including request stitching. Zero retention mode may only be used by enterprise customers.

optimize\_streaming\_latencyinteger or nullOptionalDeprecated

You can turn on latency optimizations at some cost of quality. The best possible final latency varies by model. Possible values: 0 - default mode (no latency optimizations) 1 - normal latency optimizations (about 50% of possible latency improvement of option 3) 2 - strong latency optimizations (about 75% of possible latency improvement of option 3) 3 - max latency optimizations 4 - max latency optimizations, but also with text normalizer turned off for even more latency savings (best latency, but can mispronounce eg numbers and dates).

Defaults to None.

output\_formatenumOptionalDefaults to `mp3_44100_128`

Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec\_sample\_rate\_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3\_22050\_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

### Request

This endpoint expects an object.

textstringRequired

The text that will get converted into speech.

model\_idstringOptionalDefaults to `eleven_multilingual_v2`

Identifier of the model that will be used, you can query them using GET /v1/models. The model needs to have support for text to speech, you can check this using the can\_do\_text\_to\_speech property.

language\_codestring or nullOptional

Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model and text normalization. If the model does not support provided language code, an error will be returned.

voice\_settingsobject or nullOptional

Voice settings overriding stored settings for the given voice. They are applied only on the given request.

pronunciation\_dictionary\_locatorslist of objects or nullOptional

A list of pronunciation dictionary locators (id, version\_id) to be applied to the text. They will be applied in order. You may have up to 3 locators per request

seedinteger or nullOptional

If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed. Must be integer between 0 and 4294967295.

previous\_textstring or nullOptional

The text that came before the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.

next\_textstring or nullOptional

The text that comes after the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.

previous\_request\_idslist of strings or nullOptional

A list of request\_id of the samples that were generated before this generation. Can be used to improve the speech’s continuity when splitting up a large task into multiple requests. The results will be best when the same model is used across the generations. In case both previous\_text and previous\_request\_ids is send, previous\_text will be ignored. A maximum of 3 request\_ids can be send.

next\_request\_idslist of strings or nullOptional

A list of request\_id of the samples that come after this generation. next\_request\_ids is especially useful for maintaining the speech’s continuity when regenerating a sample that has had some audio quality issues. For example, if you have generated 3 speech clips, and you want to improve clip 2, passing the request id of clip 3 as a next\_request\_id (and that of clip 1 as a previous\_request\_id) will help maintain natural flow in the combined speech. The results will be best when the same model is used across the generations. In case both next\_text and next\_request\_ids is send, next\_text will be ignored. A maximum of 3 request\_ids can be send.

apply\_text\_normalizationenumOptionalDefaults to `auto`

This parameter controls text normalization with three modes: ‘auto’, ‘on’, and ‘off’. When set to ‘auto’, the system will automatically decide whether to apply text normalization (e.g., spelling out numbers). With ‘on’, text normalization will always be applied, while with ‘off’, it will be skipped.

Allowed values:

apply\_language\_text\_normalizationbooleanOptionalDefaults to `false`

This parameter controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages. WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

use\_pvc\_as\_ivcbooleanOptionalDefaults to `false`Deprecated

If true, we won't use PVC version of the voice for the generation but the IVC version. This is a temporary workaround for higher latency in PVC versions.

### Errors

content:
# How do I use GenFM? – ElevenLabs

GenFM is available in [Studio](https://elevenlabs.io/app/studio) and [ElevenReader](https://elevenreader.io/) for iOS and Android.  It allows you to automatically create a podcast based on any document you provide.

**ElevenReader**

You can either upload a document directly to GenFM from Home, or you can use the GenFM button to generate a podcast based on a document you have already added to ElevenReader.  

**Studio**

Go to [Studio](https://elevenlabs.io/app/projects), then choose **Create a Podcast**, which will open a pop-up where you can select options for your podcast.

For the source material for your podcast, you can upload a document (EPUB, PDF, TXT, or HTML), provide a URL, or link to an existing project. Either use the suggested voice pairing or choose any voice from My Voices for the Host and Guest. Choose your model and quality setting, then click **Generate**, and the text for your podcast will be generated.

You can check and edit the text of your podcast before you convert it to audio. When you're happy, click **Convert** and the audio will be generated. Once that's done, you will be able to download the audio and listen to your new podcast. 

If you already have a script ready, we recommend using either **Start from scratch** or **Create an audiobook** as using GenFM via the **Create a Podcast** option will modify your script.

content:
# How much does GenFM cost? – ElevenLabs

**ElevenReader**

There is no cost for generating your podcast within ElevenReader. GenFM podcasts generated within ElevenReader can only be accessed within the app.

**Studio** 

There is currently no cost for generating the text of your podcast. You will be charged the regular cost of converting the text to audio, and will be notified how many credits it will cost to convert the audio before you confirm the conversion. When you create a GenFM podcast using Studio, you can edit the text before converting and can export the audio.

content:
# What is GenFM? – ElevenLabs

GenFM allows you to automatically turn any content - PDFs, articles, text, links, eBooks - into a dynamic podcast, featuring discussion with two lifelike AI co-hosts.

GenFM is available in [Studio](https://elevenlabs.io/app/studio) (Create a podcast) and in [ElevenReader](https://elevenreader.io/) on iOS and Android. 

Please note that if you already have a script ready, we recommend using "Start from scratch" or "Create an audiobook" features. As GenFM will modify your script.

content:
# What is ElevenLabs and the ElevenReader App? – ElevenLabs

ElevenLabs is an Audio AI research and deployment company. Our mission is to make content universally accessible in any language and in any voice. Our research team develops advanced AI audio models that generate realistic, versatile and contextually-aware speech, sound effects, and voices across 32 languages. Access the ElevenLabs platform here: [https://elevenlabs.io](https://elevenlabs.io/)

The ElevenReader app is a mobile app for iOS and Android offered globally that narrates text content using ElevenLabs’ advanced voice AI. Users can upload content or explore our vast library of audiobooks, short stories, and curated feeds. The app connects content owners directly with audiences, serving as a distribution channel for authors and publishers who want to enhance their written work with ElevenLabs’ AI narration.

You can download the free ElevenReader app from the [App Store](https://www.apple.com/app-store/) for iOS devices or the [Google Play Store](https://play.google.com/store) for Android devices.

content:
# How many ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI) requests can I make and can I increase it? – ElevenLabs

The concurrency limit (concurrent calls/conversations running in parallel) depends on your subscription.

Below are the current rates at the time of writing this per workspace (including all of your agents), but please note that we will likely revisit them in the future.

*   Free: 4
*   Starter: 6
*   Creator: 10
*   Pro: 20
*   Scale: 30
*   Business: 30

If you require a higher number of concurrent requests, please reach out to our Enterprise Department directly via [this webpage](https://elevenlabs.io/enterprise). We will be happy to discuss a tailor-made plan that meets your specific requirements.

You can also enable burst pricing for individual agents, which allows you to temporarily exceed your concurrency limit during high-demand periods. When enabled, your agents can handle up to 3 times your normal concurrency limit, with excess calls charged at double the standard rate. For more information, see our [documentation](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/guides/burst-pricing).

content:
# How does AI narration work? – ElevenLabs

AI narration provides on-demand, text-to-speech conversion. This provides users with high-quality, natural-sounding audio from your text content. ElevenLabs advanced AI audio models are contextually-aware — understanding both what is being said and how it should sound — allowing for the most naturally-sounding, immersive listening experience.

content:
# How is my content distributed? – ElevenLabs

Your content is available on both the iOS and Android versions of the ElevenReader app. Readers can discover it in the “Explore” section or access it directly via your unique sharing link. Currently, all content in the Explore library is free for users to access and listen to. However, we reserve the right to move content behind a paid subscription or enable authors to sell books through in-app transactions in the future.

Please note that, for now, the in-app content library “Explore” will only feature English books. We are actively working on expanding support for more languages in the future.

content:
# How do I publish a book on the ElevenReader App? – ElevenLabs

00:00

05:43

1.  Create your free account on [https://elevenreader.io/publishing](https://elevenreader.io/publishing)

2.  Upload your manuscript in EPUB, PDF, TXT, DOCX, or HTML format

3.  Review your book’s formatting in the publishing editor

4.  Preview your audio in the ElevenReader app by scanning the QR code

5.  Configure your book details, review legal terms, and enable payouts (currently US-only)

6.  Publish your book and submit it for review

7.  Share your book with your audience; view your reports and payouts

content:
# What formats are supported for publication? – ElevenLabs

Supported manuscript formats include EPUB, PDF, TXT, DOCX, and HTML. To retain images, please upload your manuscript as EPUB. It’s not currently possible to add images within the publishing editor.

The publishing editor allows for basic text formatting:

*   Paragraph
*   Chapters
*   Header 1
*   Header 2
*   Header 3

Your text may be normalized into plain text to ensure full audio compatibility, such as removing italics or underlines.

# How does ElevenReader promote published content? – ElevenLabs

ElevenReader promotes published content in the app with features on the Home and Explore pages, as well as recommendations and featured books based on popularity and personalized user recommendations. 

Additionally, selected books or authors may be highlighted in spotlight features as part of promotional activities. Authors can also independently promote their books using unique sharing links provided by the platform to direct readers to their audio publications.

# How can I delete/unlist a book that has been published? – ElevenLabs

To delete or unlist a book that has been published on the ElevenReader app, navigate to [ElevenReader.io](https://elevenreader.io/), open the 'Bookshelf' tab, and locate the book you wish to unlist. Click 'View Details' to open the book detail tab, where you will find various options. Select 'Unlist' to remove your book from the ElevenReader app content library.

Once unlisted, your book will no longer be visible to new users.

content:
# What is ElevenReader Publishing? – ElevenLabs

ElevenReader Publishing is a zero cost self-publishing program for authors and publishers to distribute content in text, enabled for on-demand AI audio narration worldwide via the ElevenReader app. Authors and publishers retain all rights to their work and control its availability.

ElevenReader Publishing is currently in beta mode, which means that features and functionalities are still being refined based on user feedback. Access may be limited and updates or changes to the platform may occur as we improve the service.

Visit the ElevenReader webpage to start publishing: [https://elevenreader.io/](https://elevenreader.io/)

content:
# What type of content can I distribute with ElevenReader Publishing? – ElevenLabs

To ensure a high-quality experience, ElevenLabs reserves the right to remove content if it does not meet our standards for customer satisfaction, if it violates the intellectual property of another, or if required by law. We actively listen to feedback from users and investigate concerns they may raise.

Authors, publishers, and selling partners are responsible for adhering to these content guidelines, which we enforce through a combination of machine learning, automation, and human review.

Content that does not meet these guidelines will be rejected or removed, and we promptly investigate any book flagged for potential noncompliance.

Please visit our full content policy here for details: [https://elevenreader.io/content-guidelines](https://elevenreader.io/content-guidelines)

content:
# What is the difference between AI audio narration and AI audiobook production? – ElevenLabs

*   **AI audio narration:** On-demand audio generation; users select voices; no upfront production or editing required.
*   **AI audiobook production:** Audio production in a full-control text and audio editing environment. This provides a high-degree of creative control, as each producer selects voice(s) and quality controls for text-to-speech output.

content:
# How is ElevenReader Publishing different from other self-publishing platforms? – ElevenLabs

ElevenReader Publishing differs in several important ways:

*   Highest-quality voice AI: The latest in advanced AI audio models for your written content.
*   Fully-integrated user experience: Combines bimodal reading and listening in the ElevenReader iOS and Android app.
*   No upfront costs for audio production: Books are enabled for on-demand AI audio narration in the ElevenReader app.
*   User personalization: Users can choose their preferred AI narrator for a fully personalized experience.

content:
# How can I preview and listen to my book in audio on the ElevenReader app? – ElevenLabs

To preview and listen to your book in audio on the ElevenReader App, navigate to **‘Preview audio in ElevenReader app’** in the top-right menu bar of the publishing editor and click it. This will open a pop-up that generates a unique QR code for your book, which you can scan with your phone’s camera (note: scan with your camera application, not via the ElevenReader app). Your phone will then open the ElevenReader app (please ensure you have the latest version installed) and display your book preview in-app.

You can select a voice and play the audio, converting your book into an audiobook. This is how your book will appear to readers in the app. If you need to make any edits to the text, return to the publishing editor, make the changes, and generate a new QR code that will lead you to the updated version of your book. Continue to edit your book  until you are satisfied with the preview. Now you can proceed to **‘Prepare for publishing’** to add additional information about your book.

metadata.title: What do I need to look out for in the publishing editor? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: When uploading a manuscript to the publishing editor, it will automatically process the file for you, splitting it into multiple...

content:
# What do I need to look out for in the publishing editor? – ElevenLabs

When uploading a manuscript to the publishing editor, it will automatically process the file for you, splitting it into multiple chapters. In the publishing editor, you can make text-based edits, add, delete, or rearrange chapters, and reformat headers. Note that not all formatting from the original manuscript will be retained to ensure compatibility with AI audio narration. We recommend reviewing the following to create a great reading and audio experience for your readers on the ElevenReader app:

*   Ensure the manuscript is complete and that all chapters have been transferred to the publishing editor. If there are significant differences from the original manuscript, consider uploading a different file type or restarting the publishing editor to manually copy over the manuscript.
*   The first chapter or page view should include the book title and author information.
*   The table of contents can be deleted, as the reader can navigate through chapters directly within the ElevenReader app.
*   Chapter titles should be formatted using Heading 1–3 for better organization.
*   Detailed information that doesn’t translate well into audio can be moved to the back of the book.
*   Validate that the text doesn’t contain page references or unwanted artifacts, which can be removed directly in the editor.
*   Remove any unnecessary blank spaces in the text for a cleaner presentation.

These steps help ensure an optimal reading and listening experience for your audience on the ElevenReader app.

content:
# Can I pre-select my preferred voice narration for my book? – ElevenLabs

No, at this time you cannot pre-select an AI voice narration for your book. Each user will have the option to choose their own AI voice narration from the voice library. If you would like to recommend a voice, we suggest adding a text note at the beginning of your book. For example: _“The author recommends listening to this book using the voice Lily.”_

content:
# How will I know when my book is published? – ElevenLabs

After submitting for review, your book will be reviewed according to our [Content Guidelines](https://elevenreader.io/content-guidelines) within 7 business days. Depending on the review outcome, your book’s status will change to either ‘Published’ or ‘Rejected.’ You will be notified via email with updates on your book’s publishing status.
