content:
# How can I share my book with my audience? – ElevenLabs

Once your book has been reviewed and approved (status changes to ‘Published’), the sharing option becomes available. To share your book with readers, family and friends, follow these steps from [https://elevenreader.io/app/publishing](https://elevenreader.io/app/publishing) 

1.  **From the ‘Bookshelf’ View:**
    *   Navigate to the main ‘Bookshelf’ view and locate the book you want to share.
    *   A right-pointing arrow will be visible; click it to open the ‘Share Your Book’ pop-up.
    *   Choose one of the sharing options displayed QR code or link
    *   The unique sharing link directs users to a web-based preview of your book, where they can add it to their ElevenReader library or listen to a preview.
    *   Example: [ElevenReader Book Preview](https://elevenreader.io/app/reader/audiobooks/80FhHw9UAvL81UlroNT4)
2.  **From the ‘View Details’ Page:**
    *   Navigate to the main ‘Bookshelf’ view and locate the book you want to share.
    *   Click ‘View Details’ to open the book details page.
    *   At the bottom, you will find options to ‘Unlist,’ ‘Share,’ or ‘View Reports.’
    *   Click ‘Share’ and copy the unique qr code or sharing link from there.

This sharing feature allows you to promote your book easily, ensuring readers can discover and engage with your content on the ElevenReader app. Please note that readers must have the app installed to access the book in full.

content:
# What is the best way to increase discoverability of my content? – ElevenLabs

Here are some effective methods to boost the discoverability of your content:

1.  Share your book with your audience using the unique sharing link.
2.  Ensure your metadata is clean, accurate, and descriptive to improve searchability.
3.  Select a high-resolution cover art design with a clear, readable title.

content:
# How can sharing influence my book discoverability? – ElevenLabs

Sharing can significantly impact your book’s discoverability. When you share your book with your audience using the unique sharing link and they add it to their library in the ElevenReader App, our algorithm rewards this engagement by improving its visibility in the ElevenReader App. Additionally, the most shared books will be highlighted in our monthly newsletter to all ElevenReader users, providing a real boost in discoverability.

content:
# Who is eligible to receive ElevenReader payouts? – ElevenLabs

**We've opened up payouts globally (previously US-only)**. This means any author, anywhere can earn $1.10 for every user that listens to at least 11 minutes of their work on ElevenReader. This is part of our [introductory payment program](https://elevenreader.io/introductory-publishing-payment-terms?utm_source=brevo&utm_campaign=ElevenReader%20Publishing%20-%20May%202025%20Update%20-%20Global%20Payouts6%20Languages&utm_medium=email), which we launched earlier this year to offer authors a way to earn from sharing their work on the ElevenReader app. To qualify, you must accept all relevant documentation, including the [Terms of Service](https://elevenlabs.io/terms-of-use), [Privacy Policy](https://elevenlabs.io/privacy-policy), and [Payment Terms](https://elevenreader.io/publishing-payment-terms), and ensure that all submitted content complies with our content guidelines.

In the self-publishing flow, you must select the option to receive engagement-based payouts and connect your Stripe account.

content:
# What are payouts for ElevenReader Publishing? – ElevenLabs

**The Payment Terms explain how you can earn and receive payouts for the content you publish through ElevenReader Publishing.** They work alongside the general Terms of Service, the [Content Guidelines](https://elevenreader.io/content-guidelines) and other applicable legal documents. In order to receive payouts, you need to agree to the Payment Terms. You can find the latest payment terms here: [https://elevenreader.io/publishing-payment-terms](https://elevenreader.io/publishing-payment-terms)

As ElevenReader Publishing is currently in beta, the Payment Terms are subject to change as we refine and improve the program. We may introduce new payout structures, expand eligibility to additional regions, or make other adjustments to enhance the experience for authors and publishers. Updates will be communicated, and we encourage participants to review the latest terms periodically.

content:
# How do I monetise my content on the app

Content submitted to the ElevenReader Publishing program will be accessible in the Explore section (content library) of the ElevenReader app. For now, this content will be available for free to app users, while authors and publishers can earn payouts based on content engagement. ElevenLabs will record the number of “unique engaged users” per content piece and payout $1.10 per unique engaged user.

metadata.title: What qualifies as a unique engaged user? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: A unique engaged user is determined based on the length of content consumed. We qualify a user as a unique engaged user if they have...

openGraph.content: What qualifies as a unique engaged user?

A unique engaged user is determined based on the length of content consumed. We qualify a user as a unique engaged user if they have added the title to their library in the ElevenReader App and lis...

content:
# What qualifies as a unique engaged user? – ElevenLabs

A unique engaged user is determined based on the length of content consumed. We qualify a user as a unique engaged user if they have added the title to their library in the ElevenReader App and listened to at least 11 minutes of that title. 

Note: To convert words to audio minutes, we apply the factor of 150 words = 1 minute

content:
# When will I get paid? – ElevenLabs

Unique engaged users are recorded consistently, whilst payments are made every two weeks. However, you must reach a minimum earnings threshold of $10 (USD) before payments are sent. If you don’t meet this threshold, your earnings will roll over to the next payment cycle.

content:
# Are there situations where my payments might be delayed or withheld? – ElevenLabs

Yes, payments can be delayed or withheld if there’s a violation of the [Terms](https://elevenreader.io/publishing-terms-of-service), invalid activity, like artificial downloads or suspicious actions, is detected, or when payments are under investigation for any other reason. If no violations are found, payments will resume.

openGraph.content: Activities such as artificially inflating engagement metrics, fraudulent transactions, or submitting misleading or unauthorized content are strictly prohibited and may result in penalties, includin...

content:
# What counts as prohibited conduct? – ElevenLabs

Activities such as artificially inflating engagement metrics, fraudulent transactions, or submitting misleading or unauthorized content are strictly prohibited and may result in penalties, including account suspension or termination. Refer to the [Content Guidelines](https://elevenreader.io/content-guidelines) for more information. 

Please note that this is a time-limited promotional payout period. In the future, the platform may transition to supporting in-app transactions and subscription-based payouts. Program participants will be notified of these changes in a timely manner.

content:
# What information is included in ElevenReader Publishing reports? – ElevenLabs

The ElevenReader Publishing program will provide you with detailed insights into how users interact with your content. In the ‘Reports’ tab on the publishing main page, you will find real-time charts that allow you to track engagement with you content from ElevenReader App content. Reports also include payout information. Below is a list of definitions to help you navigate:

Note that the top tiles in your report represent lifetime statistics, while you can set a preferred date range by selecting a custom date, last 30 days, or last 90 days. This can be applied to all titles or selected ones via the dropdown menu.

*   **Total Users:** The number of users who have added your book to their personal libraries on the ElevenReader app.
*   **Total Engaged Users:** The number of unique users who have met the engagement thresholds with your content (as defined in Section 6).
*   **Total Payouts:** The total amount of payouts received from published content. 
*   **Total Listening Duration:** The total amount of time users have spent listening to your content on a given day in hours and minutes.
*   **Listener Engagement:** The number of users progressing through your book at 25%, 50%, 75%, or 100% completion.
*   **Ratings:** Star ratings users have given your book on a scale from 1 to 5, along with the count of each rating.
*   **Average Rating:** The average star rating from all users.
*   **Top Voices:** The most frequently used voices for listening to your book.

We aim to add more analytics soon—let us know what insights you're interested in!

# Can I remove my content from ElevenReader? – ElevenLabs

Yes, you can remove content via your publishing account. Please see [How can I delete/unlist a book that has been published](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/32062944036369-How-can-I-delete-unlist-a-book-that-has-been-published) for detailed instructions. Purchased content will remain accessible to users in their personal libraries without limitation, while free content will be available for one month after removal.

content:
# What languages does ElevenLabs support for AI narration? – ElevenLabs

ElevenLabs supports over 30 languages, including English (US, UK, Australian, Canadian), Japanese, German, French, Spanish, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese, and many others. This enables authors to reach a global audience with diverse languages and accents. Please see a full list of supported languages here: [Supported Languages.](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/13313366263441-What-languages-do-you-support)

While our audio model supports multiple languages, the beta version of the ElevenReader Publishing program currently accepts content only in English, Spanish, Portuguese, Polish, German or French. However, we plan to expand support for additional languages in the future.

# How can I access usage analytics for my account? – ElevenLabs

To access [Usage Analytics](https://elevenlabs.io/app/usage) for your account, click **Developers** in the sidebar and select the **Usage** tab.

Usage Analytics lets you view all the activity on the platform for your account or Workspace.

![Screenshot 2025-08-29 at 16.37.15.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/38545177912849)

If you're in a multi-seat Workspace, the you will be able to switch between Account and Workspace. Account shows data for your individual account, while Workspace covers all accounts in your Workspace. If you’re not in a multi-seat Workspace, you will not see this option.

content:
# How can I see information about credit usage for my account? – ElevenLabs

You can access information about credit usage for your account in [Usage Analytics.](https://elevenlabs.io/app/usage) To access this, click **Developers** in the sidebar and select the **Usage** tab.

Usage Analytics lets you view all the activity on the platform for your account or Workspace.

If you're part of a multi-seat Workspace, the you will be able to switch between Account and Workspace. Account shows data for your individual account, while Workspace covers all accounts in your Workspace. If you’re not in a multi-seat Workspace, you will not see this option.

![Screenshot 2025-08-29 at 16.37.15.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/38545652599825)

You can filter your usage data in a number of different ways.

The Metric menu allows you to view usage by a range of metrics, including Credits, Minutes Used, and Seconds to First Byte.

The Breakdown menu allows you to view your usage by voice, product or API key, for example. 

Date Range allows you to select the time period to display. Options include the last 15 minutes, day, week, month, billing cycle or custom date range.

If you want to be more specific, you can use filters to show only your usage for specific voices, products, API keys along with a range of other options. For example, you can filter to see how many credits were used with a specific voice this month.

# Can I export usage data for my account? – ElevenLabs

[Usage Analytics](https://elevenlabs.io/app/usage) allows you to view and export data about credit usage for your account.

To do this, just click the “Export as CSV” button, and the data from your current view will be exported and downloaded.

![analytics-export.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/32811340470289)

For more information on the Credit Usage section of Usage Analytics, see [How can I see information about credit usage for my account?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/32811122443665-How-can-I-see-information-about-credit-usage-for-my-account)

content:
# How can I see information about API requests for my account? – ElevenLabs

You can access information about API requests for your account in Usage Analytics, under the API Requests section.

There are two tabs in Usage Analytics. The Account tab shows data for your individual account, whereas the Workspace tab covers all accounts under your Workspace. If you’re not in a multi-seat Workspace, the data will be the same across the two tabs.

![analytics-workspace-api.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/32811193768465)

In the API Requests section, you’ll find not only the total number of requests made within a specific timeframe but also the number of concurrent requests during that period.

You can view data by different time periods, for example, hour, day, month and year, and at different levels of granularity.

content:
# How many Speech to Text requests can I make and can I increase it? – ElevenLabs

The concurrency limit (concurrent requests running in parallel) depends on your subscription and whether you're using Speech to Text or Realtime Speech to Text.

Below are the current concurrency rates for Speech to Text.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |  
| **Plan** | **Speech to Text Concurrency Limit** | **Realtime Speech to Text Concurrency Limit** |
| Free | 8   | 4   |
| Starter | 12  | 6   |
| Creator | 20  | 10  |
| Pro | 40  | 20  |
| Scale | 60  | 30  |
| Business | 60  | 30  |
| Enterprise | Elevated | Elevated |

If you require a higher number of concurrent requests, please reach out to our Enterprise Department directly via [this webpage](https://elevenlabs.io/enterprise). We will be happy to discuss a tailor-made plan that meets your specific requirements.

content:
# What is Speech to Text? – ElevenLabs

Scribe is our Speech to Text model, which allows you to transcribe speech in 99 languages. It includes word-level timestamps, speaker diarization, and audio-event tagging, and can handle up to 32 speakers with a high level of accuracy.

We recommend using Scribe v1 when high-accuracy transcription is required, available via the [website](https://elevenlabs.io/app/speech-to-text) and [API](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/speech-to-text/convert). 

For real-time applications, we recommend Scribe v2 Realtime, available in our [Agents platform](https://elevenlabs.io/app/agents) and via [API](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/speech-to-text/convert). 

For more information, please see the [Speech to Text documentation.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/capabilities/speech-to-text)

metadata.title: What is a notice period? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: The notice period in the Voice Library is designed to give users advance warning if the owner of a voice they’ve saved decides to stop...

openGraph.content: The notice period in the Voice Library is designed to give users advance warning if the owner of a voice they’ve saved decides to stop sharing their voice. This ensures a smooth transition for user...

content:
# What is a notice period? – ElevenLabs

The notice period in the [Voice Library](https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-library) is designed to give users advance warning if the owner of a voice they’ve saved decides to stop sharing their voice. This ensures a smooth transition for users who rely on that voice.

When a voice actor shares their voice to the Voice Library, they can set a notice period. If no notice period is set, their voice will be removed immediately if they decide to stop sharing it, and anyone who has saved the voice will immediately lose access.

The minimum notice period is 3 months, and the maximum is 2 years. Voice owners receive increased financial rewards for selecting a longer notice period.

If a voice actor sets a notice period, users who have saved the voice will receive both email and in-app notifications when the voice owner decides to stop sharing their voice. The voice will be immediately removed from the Voice Library but will remain available for users who saved it for the duration of the notice period.

After a voice is deleted following an active notice period, it cannot be saved again because it is no longer available in the Voice Library.

Once a notice period is active, users can see in the app when the voice will be disabled. For API users, you can see this when fetching the voice using the [Get Voice endpoint](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/voices/get) by looking for `disable_at_unix` in the response. If the voice has not had its notice period active, this key will not exist or it will say `null`.

For larger operations, you can also set up a webhook notification for voice removal, ensuring you are informed as soon as a voice is removed. This can be very useful if you need to be informed as soon as the voice is removed, for example if you need to notify your customers.

![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/33538657366545)

You can set this up on your [webhooks settings page](https://elevenlabs.io/app/settings/webhooks) for your account.

content:
# Why can't I use some voices from the Voice Library? – ElevenLabs

Some voices in the Voice Library are only available to users with a paid subscription. If you're on the free plan and you try to use one of these voices, you'll see the message: "This voice is not available for free users."

This is because these voices have a credit multiplier in place. Voices with credit multipliers are not available on the free plan, but they will become available if you upgrade to a paid subscription.

When you use a voice with a credit multiplier, the credit usage will be adjusted accordingly. For example, if a voice has a 2x multiplier, generating audio will cost twice as many credits as a voice on the standard rate.

For more details on custom rates and credit multipliers, you can refer to this article: [What are custom rates and credit multipliers?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/26050485079057-What-are-custom-rates-and-credit-multipliers)

metadata.description: Before exporting your audiobook to Spotify, you need to create your audiobook in Studio. This includes selecting the voices you want to...

content:
# How do I export a Studio project to Spotify? – ElevenLabs

Before exporting your audiobook to Spotify, you need to create your audiobook in Studio. This includes selecting the voices you want to use, arranging the content, and reviewing the audio to ensure everything sounds as expected.

Once your project is finalized, you can export it for distribution.

Here are the steps you need to follow to export your Studio project to Spotify:

1.  Open your Studio project and go to **Export > Publish > Spotify.**
2.  A modal will appear explaining the process. Click **Continue** to proceed.
3.  On the **Audio Preview** screen, select which chapter you want to use for the free **audio preview** (the first 5 minutes of that chapter will be used). Please note that you won't be able to listen to the preview in this modal, but you can change which chapter is used.
4.  Adjust the narrator names that will appear in the audiobook credits. By default, voices will be labeled as "Digital Voice" followed by the name of the voice used.
5.  Once everything is set, click **Export** to generate your audiobook in MP3 format.
6.  Log in to Spotify for Authors, or create an account.
7.  In Spotify for Authors, select **New Audiobook.**
8.  Upload your MP3 files and follow the platform’s steps to distribute your audiobook.

Please note that if your project includes an Instant Voice Clone, you must verify the voice before exporting. When prompted, click **Verify**, then go to **Voices > My Voices**, select the voice, and click **Click to verify**. Once verification is complete, you can continue with the export process.

All digitally narrated titles will be clearly marked in the metadata on Spotify. The statement, “This audiobook is narrated by a digital voice.” will be added to the beginning of the audiobook description.

content:
# How do I add chapters to a Studio project? – ElevenLabs

When you create a Studio project by using the **New audiobook** option, and import a document that incudes chapters, chapters will be enabled automatically. 

To manage chapters, go to **Project options** in the top right corner, then select **Manage chapters**. This will open the **Chapters sidebar**. You can add a new chapter, using the **+** button. You can also rename and remove chapters using the **Chapter actions** (three dots) button, and drag and drop the chapters to rearrange them.  

![Screenshot 2025-11-20 at 17.57.03.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41122281704593)

If you started from a blank project, or your project didn't originally include chapters, and you now want to add chapters, you need to enable chapters from within **Project settings.** 

You can access **Project settings** by clicking on the three small lines in the upper left corner to access **Project options**, then select **Project settings**. The **Enable chapters** option can be found in the **General** settings

![Screenshot 2025-11-20 at 18.00.32.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41122270977809)

Once you've enabled chapters for your project, you can manage them from **Project options** > **Manage chapters**.

# Is content between ElevenReader and the website shared? – ElevenLabs

Yes. All your files in ElevenReader on iOS and Android sync with your ElevenReader account on web/desktop ([elevenreader.io](https://elevenreader.io/)). You can log in on any device to view and listen to your files. Some features may not yet be fully available on web.

Your ElevenReader ([elevenreader.io](https://elevenreader.io/)) account is separate from an E[l](https://elevenlabs.io/)evenLabs ([elevenlabs.io](https://elevenlabs.io/)) account.

content:
# How do I cancel my ElevenReader subscription? – ElevenLabs

You can cancel your subscription anytime through the platform where you subscribed:

**Apple users**: Go to Settings > \[your name\] > Subscriptions.

**Google Play users**: Open the Play Store > tap your profile icon > Payments & subscriptions > Subscriptions.

**Website users**: Click on your Avatar to access Account details > Manage billing.

Cancelling will prevent future charges, but you'll still have access until the end of your current billing period.

**I paid for a year, can I downgrade to monthly halfway through?**

Subscriptions cannot be downgraded mid-cycle through app stores. Once your current annual plan ends, you can choose a monthly option when resubscribing.

**Can I pause my subscription instead of cancelling?**

At this time, we don’t offer the option to pause a subscription. If you no longer wish to be charged, we recommend cancelling your current plan. You’ll continue to have access to premium features until the end of your current billing cycle, and you can resubscribe at any time.

**What happens to my files/books if I cancel my subscription?**

When you cancel your subscription, you will retain access to your files or books used during your subscription period. However, access to premium features – such as offline mode – will be restricted once the subscription ends.

**What happens to my hours if I cancel my subscription/downgrade to the free plan?** 

If you cancel your subscription, any unused hours from that plan will remain in your account for up to 12 months — but you won’t be able to use them while on the free plan. To use those hours again, you’ll need to reactivate a paid subscription at any time within the 12-month expiration window. When you downgrade, you’ll lose access to paid features like offline downloads and extended listening hours.

# How do I request a refund for my ElevenReader purchase? – ElevenLabs

If you made your purchase through the App Store or Google Play, refund requests must go through their platforms directly. We recommend checking their support pages for the steps:

[Apple Refunds](https://reportaproblem.apple.com/)

[Google Play Refunds](https://support.google.com/googleplay/workflow/9813244?visit_id=638824150999007546-2590214717&p=refundAWF&rd=1)

If you made a purchase through the website, you can request a refund by messaging our [support team](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new). Only users who have made a purchase via Stripe should contact our support directly for this purpose.

content:
# Why are Payouts not enabled for my account? – ElevenLabs

There are a couple of possible reasons why Payouts are not enabled for your account.

**You have not completed the process of setting up your Payout account**

Payouts won't be enabled for your account until you have completed the process of setting up your Payout account, including agreeing to the Terms.

To complete the process, please visit your Payouts page, and click **Complete Account Setup**, and follow the steps. 

![Screenshot 2025-04-11 153616.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/34304674986001)

If you still see **Complete Account Setup** after completing all the steps, please [contact Support](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=13145996177937) for assistance.

**You have not completed your W-8/W-9 certification**

ElevenLabs is required by U.S. tax law to collect W-8/W-9 certification from anyone who receives payments from us and is not a U.S. tax resident. For more information on this, please see our article [What is W-8/W-9 certification?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/34305213449361-What-is-W-8-W-9-certification)

Payouts will be disabled for any user who does not have a valid tax form. To resume Payouts, please visit your Payouts page and click **Manage Account**.

![Screenshot 2025-04-11 122901.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/34304508889105)

You will then see **Missing W-8/W-9 certification**. To complete your certification, click **Update** and answer the questions in the form.

![Screenshot 2025-04-11 123024.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/34304456026257)

metadata.title: What is W-8/W-9 certification? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: Why do I have to complete this form? ElevenLabs is required by U.S. tax law to collect W-8/W-9 certification from anyone who receives...

openGraph.content:
Why do I have to complete this form?
ElevenLabs is required by U.S. tax law to collect W-8/W-9 certification from anyone who receives payments from us. If you're a U.S. tax resident, you're require...

content:
# What is W-8/W-9 certification? – ElevenLabs

**Why do I have to complete this form?**

ElevenLabs is required by U.S. tax law to collect W-8/W-9 certification from anyone who receives payments from us. If you're a U.S. tax resident, you're required to submit W-9 certification. If you're not a U.S. tax resident, you're required to submit W-8 certification.

W-8/W-9 certification helps determine whether a reduced rate of tax withholding under a tax treaty applies to your payments.

**How do I complete the form?** 

The form is processed via Stripe, which walks you through the steps. We also have a detailed guide available [here](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/34305813466641-How-do-I-complete-my-W-8-W-9-certification).

**Why are you withholding tax on payments to me?** 

By default, the U.S. requires a 30% withholding tax on certain types of payments to non-U.S. persons — including royalties. However, if your country has a tax treaty with the U.S., you may be eligible for a reduced withholding rate, which you can claim on the W-8 form. If you do not claim a treaty benefit, the 30% default rate applies.

We will withhold U.S. taxes on earnings from U.S. customer usage only (not your entire payout). Earnings from non-U.S. customer usage will not be included in withholdings.

Your payout dashboard will now include a column showing tax withheld for each past payout.

**What will I receive from you?** 

Each year, we will issue a **Form 1042-S** showing the total amount we paid you and any U.S. tax we withheld. This form is sent by **March 15 of the following calendar year** and can be used when filing a U.S. tax return, if needed.

**I already pay taxes in my own country why should I also pay U.S. tax?** 

Because the income you're earning is **U.S.-sourced** (we are a U.S.-based company), we’re required to apply U.S. tax rules. You may still be able to **claim a credit or deduction** in your own country for the tax paid to the U.S., depending on your local laws.

**Can I skip the form if I don’t live in the U.S?** 

No — if you're receiving payments from us and you're not a U.S. tax resident, we are still required to collect this form to properly apply U.S. tax rules. If we don’t receive a completed W-8, we may not be able to pay you or may have to apply maximum withholding (30%).

**How do I know if my country has a tax treaty with the U.S.?** 

Stripe may also help you identify treaty eligibility as you complete the form, but you are responsible for verifying that you meet the conditions via the IRS website or a tax professional. 

**What happens if I don’t complete the form?** 

If you don’t submit a completed W-8 form, we may be required to withhold the full 30% of your earnings from US customer usage until the form is received.

content:
# Can I connect a non-Twilio phone to ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI)? – ElevenLabs

Yes — you can connect non-Twilio numbers using SIP trunking.

SIP trunking allows you to establish a direct connection between your telephony infrastructure and the ElevenLabs platform. This means you can route inbound calls from any supported phone number — even if it's not hosted on Twilio — to your agents.

Using SIP trunking, you're not limited to Twilio numbers and can integrate with existing carriers or on-premise systems. 

For more information on the setup of SIP trunking, please see our [ElevenLabs Agents documentation.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/phone-numbers/sip-trunking)

content:
# How to Update an Audio Native Player – ElevenLabs

Made edits to your website or decided to use a new voice? Here’s how to update your Audio Native player to reflect those changes:

1.  **Open the Studio project**  
    Go to the [Audio Native page](https://elevenlabs.io/app/audio-native), click the three dots next to the page you'd like to update, and select **Edit Audio**. This will open the corresponding Studio project.  
    ![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/34478880523025)
    
2.  **Make your changes**  
    Update your project as needed, such as changing the voice or editing the script.
    
3.  **Export the project as MP3**  
    Go to **Export** and export the project.  
    ![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/34478880525201)  
    _Note: This step is required for updating Audio Native, even if you don't need the MP3 file._
    
4.  **Publish the updated version**  
    Once the MP3 export is complete, go to **Export > Publish > Audio Native**.
    
5.  **Select the updated version**  
    Click **Published version** and select the newly exported version of your audio to update the embedded player.  
    ![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/34478870511121)
    

Your Audio Native player will now reflect the latest version of your project.

### **Updating the Player Title and Author**

To change the title or author shown in the Audio Native player:

These changes will be reflected automatically in the embedded player.

content:
# How do I complete my W-8/W-9 certification? – ElevenLabs

The W-8/W-9 certification will be requested when your first payout is processed. At this point, you will be notified that you need to complete the certification before you can receive your payouts.

To do this, click **My Account** and select **Payouts** to go to your Payouts page. Then click **Manage Account.**

You will see a section for W-8/W-9 certification, which will be marked **Incomplete**. Click **Update** to open the form  
![Screenshot 2025-04-11 123024.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/34305807020817)

Answer the questions on screen. The first question, "Are you considered a U.S. person for Federal Tax Purpose?", will determine whether you complete Form W-9 or Form W-8.

**Are you considered a U.S. person for Federal Tax Purpose?** 

If yes, you will be prompted to complete Form W-9:

1.  If you have not already done so, enter your SSN. 
2.  Certify the statement is accurate by signing. 
3.  Click **Submit.** 
4.  Click **Confirm** after you have reviewed your details.

If no, you will be prompted to complete Form W-8:

1.  If you are connecting a business account, it will ask you for your tax classification. Select the appropriate classification from the drop-down menu.
2.  Enter your country of citizenship. 
3.  Enter your non-U.S. Permanent residence address. 
4.  Enter your mailing address if its different from your permanent residence address. 
5.  Enter your U.S. Taxpayer ID if you have one.
6.  Enter your non-U.S. Taxpayer Identification number if you have one. This will be the Tax ID for your country of residence. 
    1.  Check the box if non-U.S. Taxpayer Identification number not legally required, for example, if your country does not issue Tax IDs. 
7.  **Tax Treaty Claims** - if you would like to claim reduced rate of withholding tax, complete this section. This will allow Eleven Labs to apply a lower rate or 0% rate (depending on your country of residence) on the payments it remits to you. 
    1.  Are you claiming a reduced rate of withholding under a tax treaty? 
        1.  Select **Yes** to claim treaty benefits or **No** if you do not want to claim reduced rate of withholding. 
            1.  Type of income: select **Royalties - Copyrights.**
            2.  Country of residence: select from the drop-down menu.
            3.  Select the tax treaty article listed in the drop-down menu.
        2.  Certify that you do not have a permanent establishment or fixed base in the U.S. (i.e. a tax presence).
        3.  Select the rate that appears in the drop-down menu - this is configured based on your country of tax residence and the tax treaty it has with the U.S.  
        4.  Read through and agree to the certification by checking the box and typing your name in the signature field. 
        5.  Click **Submit** to submit your form.

content:
# How to use tools with ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI)? – ElevenLabs

You can connect **Client Tools**, **Webhooks**, or **System Tools** to your conversational agent to enhance its functionality.

**Tools** allow your agent to interact with external data, systems, and the environment it's running in. You define which tools your agent can use, and it will automatically choose the appropriate one during a conversation.

**Webhooks** are tools that connect your assistant to external APIs. They’re ideal for tasks like:

*   Booking meetings
    
*   Accessing or updating databases
    
*   Telling the current time
    
*   And much more
    

This type of tool enables powerful real-time interactions with your services.

**Client Tools** interact directly with the user's browser or device where the agent is hosted. They’re typically used to:

*   Redirect users within your site
    
*   Send on-screen notifications
    
*   Guide users through website flows
    

These tools help deliver a more integrated and seamless user experience.

**System Tools** are built-in and ready to use as soon as your agent is created. They handle essential conversation management tasks like:

*   Ending a call
    
*   Transferring a call
    
*   Switching languages
    

These foundational tools ensure your agent can manage core interactions out of the box.

For more information on tool implementation, please see our [ElevenLabs Agents documentation.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/customization/tools)

content:
# Can I connect Twilio to ElevenLabs Agents (formerly Conversational AI)? – ElevenLabs

Yes — we offer full integration with Twilio for ElevenLabs Agents.

Twilio makes it easy to handle both inbound and outbound calls through ElevenLabs Agents. All you need is a paid Twilio account and an active Twilio phone number.

To get started:

1.  Import your Twilio number into the platform.
2.  Verify the number using your Twilio SID and Auth Token, available in the Twilio admin console.
3.  Once verified, you can assign the number to a specific agent to handle incoming or outgoing calls.

This setup allows your agent to seamlessly manage calls using your existing Twilio infrastructure.

For more information, please see our [ElevenLabs Agents documentation.](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/agents-platform/phone-numbers/twilio-integration/native-integration)

Here are a fe...

content:
# Where can I locate the reason for my call failing? – ElevenLabs

You can find all failed (and successful) calls on the Call History page.

Failed calls will display a red error message that explains why the call ended early or didn’t connect at all.

Here are a few common errors you might see:

*   **Host elevenlabs.io is not allowed to connect to this agent**  
    This error usually occurs when the agent has an allowlist enabled, but the caller is using a shared link. Removing the allowlist will resolve this issue.
    
*   **Missing required dynamic variables in \_\_\_\_\_\_ : {‘dynamic variable name’}**  
    This error typically appears when no transcript or call is generated. It means the caller didn’t configure a required dynamic variable correctly.
    
*   **Agent has exceeded its daily call limit of**  
    The agent has a preset daily call limit. This error indicates that the call failed because the limit was exceeded.
    
*   **Override is not allowed for this AI agent**  
    A simple error message indicating an attempt was made to override the prompt or other settings, but the override feature wasn’t enabled.
    
*   **Missing dynamic variable after agent transfer**  
    This means a dynamic variable required by the transferred-to agent wasn’t available, causing the call to end.
    

If your call failed and no error message is displayed, please [contact Support](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=13145996177937) for assistance.

# I subscribed, but the ElevenReader app still says I’m on the free version – ElevenLabs

If your subscription isn’t reflected in the app, try the following steps:

*   Confirm you're logged in with the same account you used to subscribe.
*   Use the Restore Purchase option in the app’s settings.
*   Restart the app and check again.

If the issue persists, please [contact Support](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new) and we will do our best to help you.

# If I uninstall the ElevenReader app, will I still be charged? – ElevenLabs

Yes. Uninstalling the app does not cancel your subscription. To avoid future charges, make sure to cancel your subscription through your app store or account settings before uninstalling.

For details on how to cancel your subscription, please see [How do I cancel my ElevenReader subscription?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/35558775639569-How-do-I-cancel-my-ElevenReader-subscription)

content:
# Do you offer student, teacher, or nonprofit discounts on ElevenReader subscriptions? – ElevenLabs

Yes. If you’re currently enrolled in an educational program or employed as an educator, and you have a valid student or educator email address, you may be eligible for our ElevenReader education discount. Eligibility depends on your institution’s domain and country. You must also be 16 or older to use ElevenReader.

You can check your eligibility and request access for your school here: [https://elevenreader.io/students](https://elevenreader.io/students) 

  
We also offer select discounts for non-profit organizations, which can be found on our [ElevenLabs Impact Program webpage](https://elevenlabs.io/impact-program).

content:
# What is Eleven v3 (Alpha)? – ElevenLabs

Eleven v3 (Alpha) is our latest and most expressive Text to Speech model, offering:

*   More human-like generations with higher quality overall
*   Support for audio tags
    *   emotions: \[sad\] \[angry\] \[happily\]
    *   delivery direction: \[whispers\] \[shouts\]
    *   non-verbal reactions: \[laughs\]\[clears throat\] \[sighs\]
*   Dialogue mode to support natural sounding audio with multiple speakers
*   Support for 70+ languages

Eleven v3 is in public alpha and is automatically available for all ElevenLabs users. It's a research preview and requires more prompt engineering than our previous models. When it works the output is breathtaking but the reliability and higher latency means it’s not suitable for real-time and conversational use cases. For these, we recommend v2.5 Turbo or Flash. We are working on the real-time version of Eleven v3.

You can generate using v3 (Alpha) via API using our [Create speech](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/text-to-speech/convert) and [Stream speech](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/text-to-speech/stream) endpoints by specifying model ID `eleven_v3`.

You can also use our [Create dialogue](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/text-to-dialogue/convert) and [Stream dialogue](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/text-to-dialogue/stream) endpoints to create a natural sounding dialogue with multiple speakers.

Visit the following resources for more information:

*   [Eleven v3 overview](https://elevenlabs.io/v3)
*   [Eleven v3 prompting guide](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/overview/capabilities/text-to-speech/best-practices#prompting-eleven-v3-alpha)

Please note that under our [Beta Services Addendum](https://elevenlabs.io/bsa), content generated using Beta Services cannot be used for any commercial purpose or in any production environment. This restriction applies to content generated using v3 (Alpha).

Eleven v3 (Alpha) offers Dialogue mode, allowing you to generate dynamic multi-speaker conversations with natural pacing, that handle interruptions, shifts in tone, and emotional cues based on conversational context.

Dialogue mode is available when you use multiple speakers via the website. 

We’ve also created new Text to Dialogue API endpoints for generating multi-speaker interactions. For more information, see our [API documentation](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/capabilities/text-to-dialogue):

content:
# How do I enable usage based billing for my account? – ElevenLabs

Here’s how to enable usage based billing for your account:

1.  Go to your [Subscription](https://elevenlabs.io/app/subscription) page.
    
2.  Click **Manage Subscription**.
    
3.  Use the **Enable usage based billing** toggle to turn usage based billing on or off.
    
4.  A pop-up will appear asking you to confirm that you want to enable usage based billing. You can set your maximum threshold here using the slider.
    
5.  Click **Confirm** when you’ve set the slider to your preferred threshold.
    
6.  You may be asked to verify your payment card before proceeding, but no charges will be made during the verification process.
    

For more information about usage based billing, please see:

*   [What is usage based billing?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/27378406011409-What-is-usage-based-billing)
*   [What is the price for usage based billing?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/24351324241937-What-is-the-price-for-usage-based-billing)

content:
# How do I change the usage based billing threshold for my account? – ElevenLabs

To allow for more control over charges, usage based billing lets you set a maximum number of additional credits that can be used on your account. You can adjust this threshold when you enable usage based billing.

Here’s how to change the threshold:

1.  Go to your **Subscription** page.
    
2.  Click **Manage Subscription**.
    
3.  Use the **Enable usage based billing** toggle to disable, then re-enable, usage based billing.
    
4.  A pop-up will appear asking you to confirm that you want to enable usage based billing. Here, you can set your maximum threshold using the slider.
    
5.  Click **Confirm** when you’ve set the slider to your preferred threshold.
    
6.  You may be asked to verify your payment card before proceeding, but no charges will be made during the verification process.
    

For more information about usage based billing, please see:

*   [What is usage based billing?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/27378406011409-What-is-usage-based-billing)
*   [What is the price for usage based billing?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/24351324241937-What-is-the-price-for-usage-based-billing)

metadata.title: How do ElevenReader hours work? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: What are ElevenReader hours, and how do they work?ElevenReader lets you listen by converting text into hours of audio. Hours are only...

openGraph.content: What are ElevenReader hours, and how do they work?ElevenReader lets you listen by converting text into hours of audio. Hours are only used when you convert your imported text into audio for the fir...

content:
# How do ElevenReader hours work? – ElevenLabs

**What are ElevenReader hours, and how do they work?**

ElevenReader lets you listen by converting text into hours of audio. Hours are only used when you convert your imported text into audio for the first time. 

*   Hours are estimated for easy reference and are based on the amount of text converted and the speed at which you listen. The faster you listen, the quicker hours will be used in generating audio.
*   Each plan's hours are based on listening at normal (1.0x) playback speed.
*   You are not charged for listening to text you’ve already converted into audio and played before.
*   You are not charged hours for listening to any of the thousands of free books or feeds in the Explore library.

**Are ElevenLabs plans the same as ElevenReader plans?**

No, ElevenReader offers its own subscription plans with an hourly system designed for listening to content on-the-go. ElevenLabs plans are different from ElevenReader plans.

**Are hours included in the free plan?**

Yes. The free plan includes 10 free hours per month, which resets weekly.

**Can I buy hours without a subscription?**

Yes. You can purchase hours without having an active subscription with hourly packs. This allows you to convert your content into audio without committing to a recurring plan. Keep in mind that some advanced features may still require a subscription to access, even if you have hours.

**If I buy a pack while on the free plan, will those be added to my two hours?**

Yes, hourly packs are additive. This means that if you buy a pack with 15 hours, for example, those will be added to your 10 hours for a total of 25 hours.

**What’s the difference between buying hours and subscribing?**

A subscription gives you ongoing access to premium features for a set period (monthly or yearly), while purchasing hours is a separate form of usage-based access for listening. Depending on your plan, you will receive a set number of hours each month, or you can purchase additional hours separately.

**Do hours expire?**

Yes. Free plans get 10 hours that expire and reset each week. Purchased hours expire 12 months after being issued for both subscriptions and one-time packs. ElevenReader will always use the hours that expire soonest first — so you get the most listening time possible.

**Will I get hours for referring a friend?**

Yes. If you refer a friend and they join through your link, both of you will earn hours upon your friend’s successful sign-up. The amount of hours you’ll earn is listed in the app.

content:
# Can I be notified when I’m running low on credits? – ElevenLabs

Yes! You can enable a feature that will send you an automated email when you’ve used a specified percentage of your credits.

Here’s how to set it up:

1.  Go to your [Subscription](https://elevenlabs.io/app/subscription) page.
    
2.  Click **Manage Subscription**.
    
3.  Use the **Enable credit usage notification** toggle to turn on this feature.
    
4.  Once enabled, you can adjust the percentage at which the notification is triggered.
    
5.  Click **Confirm** when you’ve set the slider to your preferred threshold.
    

When this feature is active, you’ll receive an email notification when you’ve reached the specified percentage of your total credits. In a multi-seat workspace, all admins will receive the email.

The Ultra plan includes “unlimited” listening time, which means up to 24 hours of audio per day at normal (1.0x) speed. This plan is not able to be shared with other accounts. Violating users will be subject to non-refundable suspending or restricting access in cases of excessive use or other misuse.

# How do offline downloads work? – ElevenLabs

Offline downloads let you convert text to audio before going offline, so you can listen without an internet connection. Plus users get 3 downloads per month, each up to 8 hours of audio. Ultra users get 10 downloads per month, also up to 8 hours each. Your downloads are stored in the app and can be played anytime, even without Wi-Fi or data.

content:
# If I subscribe at a discounted price, will it renew at the same price? – ElevenLabs

Yes — your subscription will renew at the price shown during checkout for the length of your billing cycle (monthly or annual), excluding any one-time discounts or promo codes. If pricing changes in the future, we’ll let you know in advance before your next renewal.

# How do I log in to the ElevenLabs web app if I signed up using Apple Sign-In on iOS? – ElevenLabs

You can log into the web app using your Apple Sign-In using the **Sign in with Apple** option on the log in page. 

You can also add a password to your account using the **Forgot password** option, but you will not be able to remove the link to Apple Sign-In or change the email address for your account in the future.

content:
# What is the ElevenLabs App? – ElevenLabs

The official ElevenLabs app allows you to access our most powerful voice tools from anywhere. The app is now available for both iOS and Android.

With the ElevenLabs app, you can:

*   Choose from your favorite voices and presets, all synced with your web account
*   Create voiceovers using any of our models, including our latest and most powerful model, v3, with support for emotion, tone, and pacing
*   Export audio clips and instantly drop them into tools like CapCut, iMovie, or Instagram
*   Start with 10,000 free credits per month, with full plan support for existing users
*   Seamless login for existing users — one account with your voices, your projects, anywhere

[Download on iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/elevenlabs-ai-voice-generator/id6743162587)  
[Download on Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.elevenlabs.coreapp&hl=en_US)

content:
# Can I still log in using GitHub or Facebook? – ElevenLabs

GitHub and Facebook are no longer supported as sign-in options.  
 

If you previously used GitHub or Facebook to sign in, you’ll need to switch to email and password, or - if your account uses a Gmail address - you can use Google SSO. GitHub and Facebook sign-in options no longer appear on the sign-in page but you can still update your sign-in method at any time.  
 

**Switch to email and password**

1.  Go to [https://elevenlabs.io/app/sign-in](https://elevenlabs.io/app/sign-in)
2.  Enter your ElevenLabs account email address
3.  Click **Forgot password**
4.  Use the **Change password** button in the email we send you
5.  You’ll now be able to sign in with your email and password

**Switch to Google SSO**

1.  Go to [https://elevenlabs.io/app/sign-in](https://elevenlabs.io/app/sign-in)
2.  Click **Sign in with Google**
3.  Select the Google account you want to link
4.  Confirm that you want to proceed
5.  You’ll now be able to sign in using **Sign in with Google**

**Note:** If you use Google SSO, you won’t be able to change the email address linked to your account in the future.

content:
# What is the timeline in Studio? – ElevenLabs

The timeline is a visual representation of the audio in your Studio project, displayed as a horizontal track at the bottom of the interface. It’s enabled by default in all Studio projects.

![Screenshot 2025-11-19 at 16.34.14.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41121970454161)

The timeline gives you a clearer way to view and control the structure of your content. Clips appear automatically as you enter text, and you can rearrange them by dragging and dropping to adjust the flow of your audio.

Click the **Expand/Collapse** button in the top-right of the timeline to adjust the view:

![studio-expand-collapse.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41122015188241)

Use the **Zoom In** and **Zoom Out** buttons to change how much of the timeline is visible:

![studio-zoom.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41121970455057)

content:
# Can I change my account from Apple Sign-in to email and password? – ElevenLabs

If you signed up using Apple Sign-in, it can’t be removed, and you won’t be able to change the email address linked to your account.

You can add a password and log in using your email and password, but your account will still be linked to Apple Sign-in.

To do this, select **Forgot password** on the login page and follow the steps to set a password.

This doesn’t remove Apple Sign-in or change how your account is linked. The original authentication method stays in place.

**If you delete your account, you won’t be able to reuse the same email address to sign up again.**

content:
# Can I create a Professional Voice Clone of someone else's voice? – ElevenLabs

No. You can only create a Professional Voice Clone of your own voice. Even with their consent, you cannot clone someone else’s voice. All Professional Voice Clones require a verification process to confirm that the voice belongs to you.

If someone wants to share their voice with you, they can create and verify a Professional Voice Clone on their own account, then share it with you privately using a sharing link. Learn more in our article: [How do I share a voice?](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/18644643807889-How-do-I-share-a-voice)

content:
# How can I create a voiceover for my video using Studio? – ElevenLabs

To create a voiceover for a video, you can either:

*   Start a new project using the **New video voiceover** option, which lets you upload a video file, or
*   Start a blank project using the **New blank project option** > **Video project**.

You can also add a video to an existing project: 

*   To upload a video, use the **Imports** option and either drag and drop your file, or specify the location using the **Upload file** option.
*   To add a video that you've generated using Image & Video, click **Video** in the sidebar. You'll see your previously generated videos and can import using the **+** button. 
*   You can also generate a video directly in Studio using the Video prompt, which you can access by clicking **Video** in the sidebar. 

![Screenshot 2025-11-20 at 17.44.16.png](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41121870136209)

You can:

*   **Resize the video view** by dragging the vertical divider between the video and text panes.
*   **Remove the video** by right-clicking it in the timeline and selecting **Delete**.
*   **Export your project** as a full video or audio-only file by clicking **Export**.

content:
# What is Eleven Music? – ElevenLabs

Eleven Music is our AI model for generating high-fidelity, studio-grade music from natural language prompts.

Eleven Music offers:

*   **Complete Music Tracks** from a single text prompt
*   **Granular Control** of genre, mood, style, structure, and instrumentation
*   **Vocals & Lyrics** across multiple languages, including English, Spanish, German, and Japanese
*   **Post-Generation Editing** to adjust sections and lyrics of those sections via the UI
*   **Studio-Ready Exports** in MP3 for professional workflows

### Use Cases & Commercial Use

Created in collaboration with artists, labels, publishers, and artists, Eleven Music is **cleared for broad commercial use**.

This model allows users to move beyond stock music libraries and create bespoke audio. For specific details on supported usage per tier, please refer to our [Music Terms](https://elevenlabs.io/music-terms).

### Availability & API Access

Eleven Music is available today for all users on the ElevenLabs website.

API access is available for all users on paid subscription plans. See our [API documentation](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/cookbooks/music/quickstart) for more information. 

Visit the following resources for more information:

*   [Eleven Music Product Page](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/product-guides/products/music)
*   [Eleven Music Prompting Guide](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/overview/capabilities/music/best-practices)
*   [Eleven Music Usage Terms](https://elevenlabs.io/music-terms)

content:
# What can I generate with Eleven Music? – ElevenLabs

Eleven Music is a versatile model that gives you control over many aspects of audio creation. You can generate:

*   **Full Songs with Vocals:** Create complete tracks with AI-generated lyrics and vocals in a variety of languages.
*   **Instrumental Tracks:** Generate purely instrumental music across any genre, from cinematic scores to ambient lo-fi beats.
*   **Specific Song Structures:** Use sectional generation to build a song piece by piece, defining the Intro, Verse, Chorus, Breakdown, and Outro.
*   **Music for Media:** Design custom soundtracks for videos, ads, or games by describing the scene or mood (e.g., "A high-intensity orchestral track for an epic battle scene").
*   **Genre-Specific Music:** Generate highly specific styles by including detailed prompts, such as "Traditional Spanish flamenco with palmas, nylon guitar, and Spanish-language vocals."

metadata.title: Are there any best practices for prompting Eleven Music? – ElevenLabs
metadata.description: The key to great results is a descriptive and detailed prompt. The model understands nuance, so the more information you provide, the...

openGraph.content: Are there any best practices for prompting Eleven Music?

The key to great results is a descriptive and detailed prompt. The model understands nuance, so the more information you provide, the closer the output will be to your vision. Here are some best pr...

content:
# Are there any best practices for prompting Eleven Music? – ElevenLabs

The key to great results is a descriptive and detailed prompt. The model understands nuance, so the more information you provide, the closer the output will be to your vision. Here are some best practices:

*   **Be Specific with Genre and Style:** Instead of `rock music`, try `energetic 1980s synth-pop with a driving drum machine beat and male vocals`.
*   **Layer Multiple Descriptors:** Combine mood, instrumentation, tempo, and use case.
    *   Example: `A slow, melancholic piano melody over ambient synth textures, suitable for a tragic film scene`.
*   **Define Instrumentation:** Call out the specific instruments you want to hear.
    *   Example: `Upbeat funk track with a prominent slap bass line, funky rhythm guitar, and a horn section`.
*   **Use the "Include/Exclude Styles" Feature:** Refine your output by explicitly including or excluding certain tags like acoustic, repetitive structure, or four-on-the-floor kick.
*   **Build Section by Section:** To have the most control when creating a full song, generate the Intro first. Once you're happy with that first section, you can click the "+" sign and specify the style for the next part. Then, use the "Continue the conversation..." prompt box to generate the Main Groove or Chorus, building your track piece by piece.
*   **Iterate and Refine:** If the first generation isn't perfect, don't start over! Adjust your prompt and regenerate. Small changes can have a big impact.

For a complete list of tips and examples, please see our official [Prompting Guide](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/best-practices/prompting/eleven-music).

metadata.title: How can I edit my track, add or remove sections, or change the style of a specific part in Eleven Music? – ElevenLabs

openGraph.content: How can I edit my track, add or remove sections, or change the style of a specific part in Eleven Music?

Eleven Music gives you granular control to edit your song composition without having to start over. You can easily add new parts, edit lyrics, delete sections, and even fine-tune the musical style ...

content:
# How can I edit my track, add or remove sections, or change the style of a specific part in Eleven Music? – ElevenLabs

Eleven Music gives you granular control to edit your song composition without having to start over. You can easily add new parts, edit lyrics, delete sections, and even fine-tune the musical style of each individual section.

Here’s a step-by-step guide to editing your track:

**1\. Adding a New Section**

To add a new part to your song (like a bridge, a solo, or a second verse):

*   To add a new section, there are two ways to achieve this:
*   Once you've added this new empty section, you can drag it to extend the duration, add lyrics, or a descriptive prompt (e.g., "\[energetic guitar solo\]").

**2\. Editing Lyrics or Prompts**

To change the lyrics or the instrumental prompt of any existing section:

*   Simply click inside the text box for that section (e.g., Main Groove 1, Breakdown).
*   Type your new lyrics or edit the existing prompt.

**3\. Modifying the Style of a Section**

For more advanced control, you can define specific musical elements to include or exclude from a section.

*   Hover over the section you want to edit and click the **"Edit styles of this section"** link.  
    ![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/37781973640209)
*   A "Section styles" window will pop up. Here you can:
    *   **Include styles:** Add specific musical characteristics or production details. For example: gradual filter cutoff, hi-hats fade out, long delay feedback on vocalise.
    *   **Exclude styles:** Prevent certain elements from appearing. For example: abrupt ending, new elements.
*   Click **Save** to apply these style rules to that specific section.

**4\. Deleting a Section**

If you want to remove a part of your song entirely:

*   Hover over the section you wish to remove in the timeline.  
    ![](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/37782139028497)
*   Click the delete icon, the "X", in the upper left corner of the section.
*   The section will be removed from the structure, and a notification like "Outro deleted" will briefly appear.

**5\. Generating Your Changes**

After you have made any edits—whether adding, deleting, or modifying a section—you will see a notice at the bottom that says **"Song composition has changed."**

*   Your edits will **not** take effect until you click the **Generate** button.
*   Once you click **Generate**, the model will create a new version of your track that incorporates all of your changes.

Feel free to experiment with different combinations of lyrics, styles, and structures to craft your perfect track

content:
# How do I export a Studio project to InAudio? – ElevenLabs

Before exporting your audiobook to InAudio, you need to create your audiobook in Studio. This includes selecting the voices you want to use, arranging the content, and reviewing the audio to ensure everything sounds as expected.

Once your project is finalized, you can export it for distribution.

Here are the steps you need to follow to export your Studio project to InAudio:

1.  Open your Studio project and go to **Export > Publish > InAudio.**
2.  A modal will appear explaining the process. Click **Continue** to proceed.
3.  On the **Audio Preview** screen, select which chapter you want to use for the free **audio preview** (the first 5 minutes of that chapter will be used). Please note that you won't be able to listen to the preview in this modal, but you can change which chapter is used.
4.  Adjust the narrator names that will appear in the audiobook credits. By default, voices will be labeled as "Digital Voice" followed by the name of the voice used.
5.  Once everything is set, click **Export** to generate your audiobook in LPF format.
6.  Log in to InAudio, or create an account.
7.  In InAudio, select **Start New Project.**
8.  Upload your LPF file and follow the platform’s steps to distribute your audiobook.

Please note that if your project includes an Instant Voice Clone, you must verify the voice before exporting. When prompted, click **Verify**, then go to **Voices > My Voices**, select the voice, and click **Click to verify**. Once verification is complete, you can continue with the export process.

All digitally narrated titles will be clearly marked in the metadata, and the book description will inform the listener that the title was created using digital voice narration. 

InAudio allows you to distribute your audiobook to Barnes and Noble, Rakuten, Everand, Scribd and many other more.

content:
# How do I use the Pronunciations Editor in Studio? – ElevenLabs

Sometimes you may want to specify the pronunciation of certain words, such as character or brand names, or specify how acronyms should be read. You can use the **Pronunciations editor** to add rules about how specified words should be pronounced, either using a phonetic alphabet (phoneme tags) or word substitutions (alias tags).

> **Note: Phoneme tags are only compatible with "Eleven Flash v2", "Eleven Turbo v2" and "Eleven English v1" models.**

These rules will be saved to a Pronunciation Dictionary which will be connected to your project. Whenever one of these words is encountered in a project, the AI will pronounce the word using the specified replacement.

You can add aliases and phonemes from directly within Studio by clicking the **Open pronunciations editor** button. 

![studio-pronunciations.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41121472322193)

 If you do this while you have a word selected, this word will automatically populate the input field. Otherwise, you can enter the word yourself. You can use the Play button in the Output to preview how it will sound.

![studio-pronunciations-editor.webp](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/article_attachments/41121483499537)

When you add a new rule, you can either select an existing dictionary to add the rule to, or create a new dictionary. If you add the rule to an existing dictionary, this will automatically connect it to your project.

content:
# Where do my payouts come from, and will they show as domestic or international on my bank statement? – ElevenLabs

Payouts are issued from the country of your Stripe connected account. If your external bank account is in the same country and currency, the payout typically appears as a domestic transfer. If your bank is in a different country or you’re paid in a different currency (for example, via alternative currency payouts), it may appear as an international transfer.

To gauge what to expect, compare the connected account’s country and currency with your external bank account’s country and currency—when they match, expect domestic; when they don’t, expect international.

content:
# How is my current ElevenReader subscription changing? (October 2025) – ElevenLabs

As of October 13, 2025, we’re updating ElevenReader subscription plan to be more affordable and expansive than ever:

*   Free now includes 10 hours of premium AI audio generation every month
*   Ultra now offers 24/7, unlimited listening for just $11/month or $99/year

**What happens if I’m already on Plus or Ultra?**  
No need to worry. Here’s how things are changing:

*   **Plus is merging into Ultra — you’ll automatically receive all Ultra perks.** At the end of your next billing cycle (monthly or annual) you can renew and continue enjoying these perks while still on your Plus plan. You can also cancel your current billing plan (Plus) and re-subscribe to Ultra on your next subscription term. Either way, your Plus subscription will receive all Ultra perks.
*   **Ultra subscribers will enjoy a price discount on their future subscription terms. You will notice your monthly price decrease on your next billing term.**
*   **Ultra Annual subscribers get a special thank you**. Your next year of ElevenReader Ultra will be entirely free and granted to your with a discount code. We want to sincerely thank you for being an early supporter of ElevenReader. 

If you have any questions or issues with your subscription plan, you can [contact support with any questions](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=13145996177937).

content:
# Are there any limits on the types of content I can listen to in ElevenReader? – ElevenLabs

Yes, content uploaded to ElevenReader for listening is governed by the [ElevenLabs Prohibited Use Policy](https://elevenlabs.io/use-policy). This policy supplements our Terms of Service and applies to your access and use of our services, and is intended to ensure ElevenLabs’ services are not used for illicit purposes.

We use a combination of automated systems, user reports, and human review to assess material and usage that may violate this Policy. For users who violate this Policy, we may remove the violating material, and/or suspend your access to and use of our Services.

Some specific examples of content that may violate this policy include:

1.  Do not threaten child safety.
2.  Do not engage in illegal behavior.
3.  Do not create violent, hateful, or harassing material outside of fictional contexts. 

If you believe your account has been incorrectly banned, or your material has been incorrectly removed, you can let us know by contacting us [here](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=13145996177937).

content:
# How do credits work on the ElevenLabs iOS or Android app? – ElevenLabs

Credits give you flexibility to power your listening. For our Eleven v3 and Multilingual v2 models one text character will cost you one credit. For the Turbo v2.5 model one character will cost you 0.5 credits.

**Why do we use credits?** 

We believe it’s fair for users as it gives you maximum flexibility. You only get charged credits when you generate content. 

**How can I get more credits for the ElevenLabs iOS or Android app?**

Subscribe to our Creator monthly or annual plans for the best savings on credits, or for more options you can login and upgrade at [elevenlabs.io](http://elevenlabs.io/). You can use your subscription across our website, iOS and Android apps. 

If you subscribed through the mobile app, it isn't possible to enable usage based billing. If you want access to this feature, you'll need to switch to a website subscription. For more information on how to manage your subscription in the app, see [Managing Your ElevenLabs Subscription (Mobile App vs. Website)](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/40570893074449-Managing-Your-ElevenLabs-Subscription-Mobile-App-vs-Website)

**When do credits expire in the ElevenLabs iOS or Android app?**

If you have a subscription, you will automatically rollover unused credits up to twice your usual monthly quota, provided you remain on the same subscription. If you cancel or downgrade your subscription, you will lose any unused credits when that takes effect at the end of your billing cycle.

# How do I request a refund or cancel my subscription in the ElevenLabs iOS or Android app? – ElevenLabs

To cancel your plan, you can visit your Account page in the app and cancel using the Apple or Google “Manage subscriptions” settings. 

You can request a refund directly through the App Store or Play Store: [Visit Apple's App Store support](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118223) or [visit Google's Play Store support](https://support.google.com/googleplay/answer/15574897) to process your in-app purchase refund.
